title: Phased builds — JIT per-phase decompose + inter-phase replan
description: Make build phases a first-class, opt-in feature: /vision identifies natural phase boundaries (skeleton-first), then the pipeline loops decompose→build→retro→replan one phase at a time. Phase isolation comes from just-in-time decomposition (next phase's beads don't exist until the current phase is built + replanned), not a graph gate. Single-phase by default, backward-compatible (schemaVersion stays 2). Design of record: docs/PHASED_BUILD_PROPOSAL.md. Also fixes the vanishing-deferred-must-have bug (vision.js:672) and lights up decompose verifier C's dead deferred branch.

title: Add /split skill: take an oversized bead + seam, produce N atomic child beads with dep rewiring
description:
WHY: /quality-pass and /compose's sizing audit detect oversized beads but the human has to manually re-pour formulas or edit plan.md. For "decompose into smallest atomic parts" to be mechanical rather than aspirational, splitting needs to be a one-command operation — currently the biggest friction point between /compose and /loop /build-next.

WHAT TO DO:
- Add `skills/split/SKILL.md` with frontmatter (name=split, triggers on "/split", "split this bead", "atomize bead X").
- Inputs: bead ID + optional seam description. If seam omitted, refuse with a prompt — splitting without a seam is just guessing.
- Process:
  1. Read source bead (`bd show $id --json`). Read its parent epic, dependents, dependencies.
  2. From the seam + AC, propose N child beads with: distinct titles, derived ACs, file/path ownership boundaries (basis for the filesTouched work), and a fanout dep graph (sequential = child_n+1 depends on child_n; else parallel siblings).
  3. Print the proposed split. Do NOT mutate without explicit user confirmation.
  4. On confirm: `bd create --parent <epic>` each child; `bd dep add` for child↔child and child↔external (preserve incoming/outgoing edges from the source bead); close the source bead with reason "superseded by <ids>" (verify with `bd update --help` whether a `superseded` status exists; if not, use closed + reason).
  5. Re-validate `bd dep cycles`.
- Read-only escape: if a quality-pass-style audit shows the proposed children would still be oversized (ACs>6 etc.), refuse and recommend a different seam.

Link from /quality-pass output: skills/quality-pass/SKILL.md should mention /split as the natural follow-on for beads scoring <95 on sizing penalties.

ACCEPTANCE (self-verifiable):
- `skills/split/SKILL.md` exists with frontmatter `name: split`.
- File contains literal phrases "seam", "confirm before mutate", "bd dep add", "superseded".
- `skills/quality-pass/SKILL.md` mentions `/split` as the follow-on for oversized beads.
- README.md pipeline section names /split alongside /compose and /quality-pass.
- A junction/symlink is created in ~/.claude/skills/split (or installer task autonomous-build-t9i handles it).

title: Add filesTouched bead metadata + conflict-aware dispatch filter in /build-batch
description:
WHY: /build-batch currently relies on the post-merge gate to catch sibling-bead file conflicts (see skills/build-batch/SKILL.md line 330 — explicit concession). Every collision burns a full worker run + a gate run. We already write testPlanFile via metadata in compose Step 4d (autonomous-build-5au); extending the same mechanism to filesTouched is cheap and turns the parallelism from "hopefully non-overlapping" into actually conflict-free.

WHAT TO DO:
- Formula schema: add a `files` array under `[steps.*]` blocks in formulas/*.formula.toml (paths the step's bead may touch, including globs). Variable-substituted the same way `testPlan.file` is. Document in formulas/README.md with an example.
- compose: in Step 4d (where testPlan metadata is written), additionally parse the step's `files` array and write to bead metadata as `filesTouched` (string array). Apply variable substitution. If a step has no `files`, skip — same pattern as testPlan.
- build-batch Dispatch-Bead: read `$bead.metadata.filesTouched`. Reject the candidate if its glob-expanded set intersects any in-flight pipeline's filesTouched, OR any bead currently in $mergeQueue / $mergeInFlight. Log the skip ("[SKIP] $id deferred — would conflict with $other on $sharedPaths"). Pick the next non-conflicting candidate. If none, fall through to the normal "no free slot" state.
- Beads with no filesTouched declared: fall back to current dispatch behavior, but print a one-line warning ("[WARN] $id has no filesTouched — relying on post-merge gate; consider adding `files` to the formula step") so formula gaps surface.
- Update existing formulas (crud-feature, app-skeleton) to declare `files` per step.
- Remove the line-330 acknowledgement in build-batch/SKILL.md (or update it to describe the new behavior).

ACCEPTANCE (self-verifiable):
- formulas/crud-feature.formula.toml and formulas/app-skeleton.formula.toml both contain at least one `files = [...]` entry under a `[steps.*]` block.
- formulas/README.md documents the `files` field with an example.
- skills/compose/SKILL.md describes the filesTouched metadata write (in Step 4d or a new step).
- skills/build-batch/SKILL.md Dispatch-Bead section includes the conflict filter — the literal phrase "filesTouched" must appear in the dispatch decision logic.
- skills/build-batch/SKILL.md no longer contains the original "post-merge gate is what catches the conflict" sentence (or has been updated to describe the new filter).

description:

A typo silently drops a feature, caught only by the end-of-pour coverage check. A machine-readable mirror eliminates the parse fragility without removing the human-readable plan.md.

title: Polish orchestration: explicit path staging, remote-aware pull, builder-driven completion signal
description:
Bundle of three small orchestration fixes called out during workflow review. Independent of the larger improvements; file together for cohesion.

1. **Explicit path staging in build-next Step 9.**
   skills/build-next/SKILL.md line 163 uses `git add -A`, AFTER the gate's pre-commit safety scan has already run. Anything created between the gate and the commit (kickoff receipts mishandled, scratch files, an .env an agent created to test something) is unscreened by the safety check. Replace with explicit staging derived from the kickoff's ownership boundaries — `target/jankurai/kickoff-$id.json` enumerates the files the bead may touch. Stage those paths only.

2. **Remote-aware pull in build-batch Merge-And-Close.**
   skills/build-batch/SKILL.md line 245: `git pull --ff-only origin main 2>&1 | Out-Null` — the comment says "ignore if no remote configured" but the code just suppresses the error. The subsequent merge still runs against the (potentially stale) local main. Replace with:
   ```powershell
   if ((git remote) -ne $null -and (git remote) -ne '') {
     git pull --ff-only origin main
     if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { <abort merge, block bead> }
   }
   ```

3. **Builder-driven completion signal in build-batch poll loop.**
   skills/build-batch/SKILL.md line 164 polls every 10s × N workers × ~30 min wall = a lot of Opus tokens spent reading idle TaskOutput. The marker is currently parsed from worker stdout. Two cheaper alternatives:
   - Workers write `<worktree>/.bd-build-complete.json` when done (same JSON content as the current BUILD_COMPLETE marker). Orchestrator watches the filesystem (PowerShell `Register-ObjectEvent` on FileSystemWatcher, or the Monitor tool with the marker path).
   - Or: have the worker `Set-Content` a sentinel and the orchestrator uses a longer sleep (60s) — less elegant but a 6× reduction without infrastructure work.
   The first option is the "real" fix; the second is the quick-win fallback if the first hits tool-availability snags.

ACCEPTANCE (self-verifiable):
- skills/build-next/SKILL.md Step 9 no longer contains the literal string "git add -A"; contains language about staging from kickoff ownership / explicit paths.
- skills/build-batch/SKILL.md Merge-And-Close gates the pull with a `git remote` check (literal phrase "git remote" near the pull command).
- skills/build-batch/SKILL.md poll loop describes a marker-file / filesystem-watch mechanism (literal phrase ".bd-build-complete" or `FileSystemWatcher`), OR documents the 60s-sleep fallback with a TODO referencing the marker-file approach.
notes: Freshness note (2026-05-28): line numbers drifted (build-next git add -A is line 162 not 163; build-batch pull is line 301 not 245; sleep is line 176 not 164) but all three constructs are unchanged and still match the bead's diagnosis.

title: Write formulas/auth-email-password.formula.toml
description:
WHY: every non-trivial app needs auth, and crud-feature shoehorns are wrong shape (auth is cross-cutting, not entity-CRUD). plan.md picks for "Habits with login" currently have nowhere to land.

WHAT TO DO: write `formulas/auth-email-password.formula.toml` covering email + password auth:

Variables:
- `hash_strategy` (default `argon2`; alternatives `bcrypt`, `scrypt`)
- `session_strategy` (default `server-session`; alternative `jwt`)
- `session_ttl_minutes` (default `1440`)
- `require_email_verification` (default `false`)

Steps (each as a `[[steps]]` block with `files = [...]` and where applicable `[steps.testPlan]`):
1. **schema** — users + sessions tables (or sessions store keyed by session_strategy). Files: migrations/*_auth.*, models/User.*, models/Session.*.
2. **password-hashing** — password hash + verify functions per hash_strategy. Files: services/auth/passwords.*, tests/services/auth/passwords.*. testPlan: hash deterministic check, verify roundtrip, timing-safe compare.
3. **register-endpoint** — POST /auth/register. Files: routes/auth/register.*, tests/endpoints/auth/register.*. testPlan: 201 on valid, 400 on missing fields, 409 on duplicate email.
4. **login-endpoint** — POST /auth/login. Files: routes/auth/login.*, tests/endpoints/auth/login.*. testPlan: 200 + session cookie/token on valid, 401 on bad password, 401 on unknown user.
5. **session-middleware** — request gate that resolves session → user. Files: middleware/session.*, tests/middleware/session.*. testPlan: anonymous request → 401; valid session → user populated; expired → 401.
6. **logout-endpoint** — POST /auth/logout. Files: routes/auth/logout.*, tests/endpoints/auth/logout.*. testPlan: invalidates session; subsequent request → 401.

Important: every step must include a `labels = ["touches-auth"]` declaration. /build-next escalates touches-auth as a blocker (per docs/ESCALATION_RULES.md) — the formula SHOULD make every auth-related step block on first encounter so the human confirms the cryptographic choices. The escalation pre-check is the design, not a bug.

Do NOT include OAuth, magic-link, MFA, or password-reset in this v1 formula. Each of those is its own follow-on formula (auth-oauth, auth-magic-link, auth-mfa, auth-password-reset). Bias toward the smallest possible "users can sign up and log in" cluster.

ACCEPTANCE (self-verifiable):
- `formulas/auth-email-password.formula.toml` exists at repo root under formulas/.
- `bd formula list` includes `auth-email-password` (verify by grepping the output).
- `bd cook auth-email-password --mode=runtime --var hash_strategy=argon2 --var session_strategy=server-session --var session_ttl_minutes=1440 --var require_email_verification=false` exits 0 and emits a parseable JSON proto.
- Every `[[steps]]` block declares both `files = [...]` and `labels = ["touches-auth"]`.
- Steps that ship code with tests declare a `[steps.testPlan]` sub-table.
- `formulas/README.md` does not need a new section — the field schema is already documented; just keep this formula consistent with the conventions there.

title: Write formulas/page.formula.toml
description:
WHY: many features are "add a page" — a settings screen, an about page, a dashboard widget container. crud-feature is overkill (no entity); ad-hoc bd create is forbidden. Need a formula for the smallest UI unit.

WHAT TO DO: write `formulas/page.formula.toml` for a single named page with optional data fetch:

Variables:
- `page_name` (required, e.g. "Settings", "Dashboard")
- `route_path` (required, e.g. "/settings", "/dashboard")
- `fetches_data` (default `false`) — if true, page gets a data hook + endpoint dependency
- `data_endpoint_var` (optional) — the data endpoint to fetch from (only required when fetches_data=true)

Steps:
1. **route** — register the route in the framework's router. Files: routes/index.*, src/router.*, app/{{route_path}}/page.*. Small step, no testPlan.
2. **component** — the page component, list view, or container. Files: pages/{{page_name}}.*, components/pages/{{page_name}}.*, app/{{route_path}}/page.*.
3. **data-hook** (skipped when fetches_data=false) — fetches data from data_endpoint_var. Files: hooks/use{{page_name}}.*, src/hooks/use{{page_name}}.*. testPlan: loading state, error state, success state with data.
4. **page-test** — component-level test. Files: tests/ui/pages/{{page_name}}.*. testPlan: renders without crashing; if fetches_data: shows loading/error/data states.

Important:
- The skip-at-pour-time pattern (used in crud-feature's UI steps with `include_ui`) applies here too: if `fetches_data=false`, the data-hook step is skipped. Document the convention.
- The formula must NOT bake in a specific frontend framework — use multi-glob files lists (pages/X.* OR app/X/page.* OR src/pages/X.*) so it works for Next.js, Vite-React, SvelteKit equally.

ACCEPTANCE (self-verifiable):
- `formulas/page.formula.toml` exists.
- `bd formula list` includes `page`.
- `bd cook page --mode=runtime --var page_name=Settings --var route_path=/settings --var fetches_data=false` exits 0.
- `bd cook page --mode=runtime --var page_name=Dashboard --var route_path=/dashboard --var fetches_data=true --var data_endpoint_var=dashboard-summary` exits 0 AND the proto includes the data-hook step (verify by grepping the JSON).

title: Write formulas/migration.formula.toml
description:
WHY: standalone schema changes (add column, add index, backfill) don't fit crud-feature's "full slice" shape. Currently the only escape is reaching for bd create, which is forbidden. Many plans need one-off migrations as their own atomic unit.

WHAT TO DO: write `formulas/migration.formula.toml` for a single schema migration with no app-code changes:

Variables:
- `migration_name` (required, e.g. "add-archived-flag-to-habits")
- `up_outline` (required) — human-readable description of what `up` does (e.g. "ALTER TABLE habits ADD COLUMN archived BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false")
- `down_outline` (required) — what the rollback does
- `touches_existing_data` (default `false`) — if true, this migration runs against rows that exist in deployed environments

Steps:
1. **write-migration** — author the up + down migration files. Files: migrations/*_{{migration_name}}.*, db/migrations/*_{{migration_name}}.*. No testPlan — the next two steps verify it.
2. **apply-fresh** — apply the migration against a fresh DB. Files: tests/migrations/{{migration_name}}.apply.*, scripts/migrate.*. testPlan: migration runs to completion against fresh DB; schema-check confirms target state.
3. **rollback-test** — apply then revert; verify no orphaned objects. Files: tests/migrations/{{migration_name}}.rollback.*. testPlan: up then down leaves the schema identical to pre-up; column/table counts match.

Important:
- When `touches_existing_data=true`, the write-migration step MUST include a `labels = ["needs-decision"]` declaration so /build-next escalates per docs/ESCALATION_RULES.md. The human must confirm the backfill strategy, locking implications, and downtime budget BEFORE the migration is written.
- Do NOT support data backfills in this v1 formula — those need their own formula (`migration-with-backfill`) because they need batched-write strategies, progress reporting, and resumability.
- The formula targets one migration. Multi-migration cluster changes (e.g. extracting a new table from a column) belong in a multi-step formula or a hand-decomposed sub-tree via /split.

ACCEPTANCE (self-verifiable):
- `formulas/migration.formula.toml` exists.
- `bd formula list` includes `migration`.

title: Write formulas/background-job.formula.toml
description:
WHY: scheduled cleanups, queue-driven webhooks, batched recomputes — background jobs are common but have no formula. crud-feature has no concept of "non-request-driven work." Without a formula, planners default to "we'll figure it out later," which means /build-next never builds it.

WHAT TO DO: write `formulas/background-job.formula.toml` for a single scheduled or queue-triggered job:

Variables:
- `job_name` (required, e.g. "daily-habit-rollup", "process-webhook")
- `trigger_type` (required: `schedule` | `queue`)
- `schedule` (only required when trigger_type=schedule; cron expression or interval, e.g. "0 3 * * *")
- `queue_name` (only required when trigger_type=queue)
- `idempotent` (default `true`) — declares whether the job tolerates re-runs; the formula uses this to decide whether to scaffold an idempotency key check

Steps:
1. **handler** — the job function itself. Files: jobs/{{job_name}}.*, src/jobs/{{job_name}}.*.
2. **trigger-wiring** — register the job with the scheduler (cron config, schedule registration) OR the queue subscription. Files: config/jobs.*, src/scheduler.*, src/workers/{{job_name}}.*. No testPlan — wiring is verified by integration test in the next step.
3. **idempotency-guard** (skipped when idempotent=true, i.e. when the handler is naturally idempotent and needs no key check) — adds a per-invocation key check. Files: jobs/{{job_name}}.guard.*, services/idempotency.*. testPlan: duplicate invocation returns early; first invocation runs.
4. **handler-test** — unit test for the handler function. Files: tests/jobs/{{job_name}}.*. testPlan: handler runs to completion on representative input; handler is observably idempotent under repeated invocation if idempotent=true; on failure handler emits the expected error signal (re-raise for queue retry, log + skip for cron).

Important:
- This formula targets one job. Multi-stage pipelines (e.g. extract → transform → load workflows) need a different formula or a hand-decomposed sub-tree.
- Do NOT scaffold a worker process here. If the app doesn't have a worker, that's a separate `app-skeleton`-level concern.
- The handler-test must hit the handler directly with a fake input, not go through the trigger. Trigger-side integration tests (cron firing, queue draining) are out of scope for v1.

ACCEPTANCE (self-verifiable):
- `formulas/background-job.formula.toml` exists.
- `bd formula list` includes `background-job`.
- `bd cook background-job --mode=runtime --var job_name=daily-habit-rollup --var trigger_type=schedule --var schedule="0 3 * * *" --var idempotent=true` exits 0.
- `bd cook background-job --mode=runtime --var job_name=process-webhook --var trigger_type=queue --var queue_name=webhooks --var idempotent=false` exits 0 AND the proto INCLUDES the idempotency-guard step (verify by grepping).
- Every `[[steps]]` block declares `files = [...]`.
- handler + handler-test + idempotency-guard (when present) declare `[steps.testPlan]`.

title: Write formulas/integration-http.formula.toml
description:
WHY: every app eventually integrates with an outbound HTTP API (payment, email, third-party SaaS). Currently no formula covers this; the secret-handling step would touch escalation rules. A formula that structures the work so non-secret pieces can ship in parallel while secret handling waits for human input is exactly the "smallest atomic parts + orchestrate" goal.

WHAT TO DO: write `formulas/integration-http.formula.toml` for an outbound HTTP client integration:

Variables:
- `integration_name` (required, e.g. "stripe", "sendgrid", "internal-billing")
- `base_url` (required)
- `auth_strategy` (required: `api-key` | `oauth-client-credentials` | `hmac-signed` | `none`)
- `retry_policy` (default `"exponential-3-attempts"`; alternatives `"linear-5-attempts"`, `"none"`)
- `timeout_seconds` (default `30`)

Steps:
1. **client** — the HTTP client class/module with retry policy applied. No secret handling here yet — accepts credentials as a constructor/init parameter. Files: services/integrations/{{integration_name}}/client.*, src/integrations/{{integration_name}}/client.*. testPlan: client constructs with sample creds; client honors retry_policy on 5xx; client respects timeout_seconds.
2. **secret-handling** — wires credentials from env/secret store into client construction. Files: config/secrets/{{integration_name}}.*, services/secrets.*. **MUST declare `labels = ["touches-secrets"]`** so /build-next escalates per docs/ESCALATION_RULES.md. The human decides the secret store, rotation policy, and access-control before this step runs.
3. **happy-path-test** — integration test against a mocked HTTP server or replay fixtures. Files: tests/integrations/{{integration_name}}/happy.*. testPlan: representative successful call returns parsed response; client makes the expected requests in the expected order.
4. **error-path-test** — integration test for retry behavior + error surfaces. Files: tests/integrations/{{integration_name}}/errors.*. testPlan: 5xx triggers retry per policy; 4xx surfaces typed error; timeout surfaces typed error; final failure after retries surfaces typed error.

Important:
- Steps 1, 3, 4 are independent of secret-handling and SHIP IN PARALLEL with secret-handling waiting. The formula's needs= declarations should NOT make step 1 depend on step 2 — only `secret-handling` blocks on human input.
- Do NOT mock secret handling in tests. Tests construct the client with hardcoded fake credentials directly; secret handling is a separate plumbing concern verified once by /build-next's gate (or manually after the human confirms).
- Do NOT add request signing, OAuth refresh flows, or rate-limit handling in v1. Each is its own follow-on formula (`integration-http-oauth-refresh`, `integration-http-rate-limited`).

ACCEPTANCE (self-verifiable):
- `formulas/integration-http.formula.toml` exists.
- `bd formula list` includes `integration-http`.
- `bd cook integration-http --mode=runtime --var integration_name=stripe --var base_url=https://api.stripe.com --var auth_strategy=api-key --var retry_policy=exponential-3-attempts --var timeout_seconds=30` exits 0.
- secret-handling step declares `labels = ["touches-secrets"]` (verify by grepping the proto JSON for `touches-secrets`).
- Step `needs:` graph allows client / happy-path-test / error-path-test to run independently of secret-handling (verify by inspecting `needs` arrays — none of these three should list `secret-handling` as a need).
- Every `[[steps]]` block declares `files = [...]`.
- client + happy-path-test + error-path-test declare `[steps.testPlan]`.

title: /build-next: read app tenets.md before making judgment calls
description: Now that /vision produces a per-app tenets.md (autonomous-build-yxn), the builder needs to actually consult it. Update skills/build-next/SKILL.md so the builder reads tenets.md as part of its context-kickoff phase, before any judgment-call moment. title: bd/Dolt backend SIGSEGV on smbuild .beads pour/list -> wipes DB, blocks any real /decompose
description:
## Summary
During /decompose pours against a freshly-init'd smbuild `.beads/` (Dolt backend, bd 0.55.4), the Dolt embedded SQL engine SIGSEGV'd (`doltdb.(*DoltDB).SetCrashOnFatalError` nil-pointer), corrupting and tearing down the entire `.beads/` dir (dolt/, dolt-access.lock, .local_version). Subsequent `bd list`/`bd mol pour` crash or report "no beads database found". Retried 8+ times, never succeeded. This failed 2 pours (Tenant-boot chokepoint, Audit chain) AND would break ANY real (non-dry) /decompose, since the DAG cannot be persisted/queried.

## Notes / scope
- autonomous-build's own Dolt-backed `.beads` is healthy (bd info/ready/create work), so this appears smbuild-local (corrupt fresh init) and/or a bd-0.55.4 Dolt edge case.
- Possible remediations to evaluate: `rm -rf smbuild/.beads/dolt && bd init` (re-init), switch smbuild beads to the jsonl backend, or pin/upgrade bd. NOT self-fixing this — it is an environment/tooling decision.

## Provenance
Found by smbuild plan-creation co-evo dry-run /decompose. Environment/infra blocker (escalate-class).
notes:
BLOCKED by /build-next (meta): escalate-class environment/tooling decision, not a self-fixable in-repo change. (1) The bead's own scope says 'NOT self-fixing this — it is an environment/tooling decision' and tags it 'Environment/infra blocker (escalate-class)'. (2) No acceptance criteria to self-verify. (3) It affects smbuild's .beads (Dolt SIGSEGV on fresh init, bd 0.55.4); autonomous-build's own .beads is healthy, so there is no autonomous-build code/skill/formula/doc fix. Human decision needed among the documented remediations: 'rm -rf smbuild/.beads/dolt && bd init' (re-init), switch smbuild beads to the jsonl backend, or pin/upgrade bd. The loop must not run a destructive rm-rf or backend switch on a guess.
2026-05-29 (smbuild plan-loop, manual re-decompose): the 'blocks ANY real /decompose' premise is DISPROVEN — a clean re-run poured 209 real beads (epic smbuild-8ko, honest NEEDS-FIX), and direct bd health checks (stats/formula list/create/delete) all pass on smbuild. The SIGSEGV reproduces specifically when bd commands run against a NON-EXISTENT .beads DB. That no-DB state was created by the args-token-scan footgun (autonomous-build-3ch): a wrapper prompt saying 'do NOT pass --no-file' set dryRun=true, which skips the Phase-2 bootstrap bd init, leaving no DB for the dry-run pours to hit. Net: most of 2t7 is a symptom of 3ch; the residual real nit is that bd/Dolt SIGSEGVs (nil-ptr) instead of erroring cleanly when no DB exists. Suggest re-scoping 2t7 to that bd-robustness nit (or upstreaming to bd), and fixing 3ch to prevent the no-DB state in the first place.
CROSS-REF (from 3ch, fixed 2026-05-29): the 'blocks ANY real /decompose' SIGSEGV framing is largely a SYMPTOM of 3ch — parseArgs flipped dryRun=true from a flag token in wrapper prose, so no .beads DB was created and bd commands then SIGSEGV'd against a non-existent DB. 3ch is now fixed (flags honored only as leading tokens; smoke test tests/decompose/parseargs.test.mjs). Re-scope 2t7: re-test a REAL pour with clean args + pre-existing .beads before treating the Dolt SIGSEGV as an independent infra fault.

title: decompose.js parseArgs token-scans the whole args string for --no-file/--auto-bless — prose mention (even a negation) silently flips dryRun/autoBless
description:
## Summary
`workflows/decompose.js` `parseArgs` does `s.trim().split(/\s+/)` and then scans EVERY whitespace token for `--no-file` (-> dryRun=true) and `--auto-bless` (-> autoBless=true). It does not require these to be leading/standalone flags — any occurrence anywhere in the args string matches. When `/decompose` is invoked with a natural-language wrapper (headless `claude --print "/decompose <prose>"`, or Workflow `args`), the prose becomes the args string, so a literal `--no-file`/`--auto-bless` token in the prose — **even inside a negation like "do NOT pass --no-file"** — flips the flag on.

## Repro (smbuild, 2026-05-29)
A "real" re-decompose of smbuild was launched with a wrapper prompt that said: *"Run ... as a REAL pour — do NOT pass --no-file and do NOT pass --auto-bless."* The workflow parsed `dryRun=true` (and `autoBless=true`), skipped the Phase-2 bootstrap `bd init` (gated on `NOT dryRun`), created no `.beads` DB, and the dry-run pours then threw `bd formula list` SIGSEGV / "no beads database found" -> a degraded report `# (DRY RUN) ... Verdict: NEEDS-FIX` (coverage-gap), `appEpic <dry-run>`, 0 persisted beads. A clean retry with NO flag tokens in the prose produced the genuine result (209 persisted beads, real epic smbuild-8ko, honest NEEDS-FIX).

## Why it matters
- Silently inverts the most consequential mode bit of the load-bearing pre-build stage.
- The failure is self-camouflaging: the resulting no-DB state throws bd/Dolt errors that look like an infra fault (this was initially mis-attributed to autonomous-build-2t7). **2t7's "blocks ANY real /decompose" framing is largely this symptom** — a real pour works fine (209 beads) once args are clean and `.beads` pre-exists; the SIGSEGV reproduces specifically when bd commands run against a non-existent DB that a dryRun=true run never created. Worth re-scoping 2t7 in light of this.

## Fix direction
Honor `--no-file`/`--auto-bless` only as deliberate, standalone leading tokens (e.g. stop scanning at the first non-flag token), or take them from a structured args field rather than free-scanning arbitrary prose. Add a smoke assertion: a wrapper string containing the literal token inside prose must NOT set the flag.

Surfaced by the smbuild plan-loop dogfood run on 2026-05-29.
acceptance_criteria: parseArgs no longer flips dryRun/autoBless from a flag token embedded anywhere in the args string; the flags are honored only as standalone intentional tokens (or from a controlled args field). A /decompose wrapper prompt whose prose contains 'do NOT pass --no-file' runs a REAL pour (dryRun=false).

title: /vision picks generic formulas, not stack-native variants, for a Rust/gRPC app
description:
REPRO (smbuild /decompose run 2, 2026-05-28):
1. docs/DEFAULT_STACK.md pins the stack to Rust (core/services = Rust; contracts = gRPC/tonic/ConnectRPC per plan).
2. /vision produced plan.lock.json that bound the GENERIC formulas to Rust backend features: Repo skeleton, Closed grammar, OpenTelemetry, Terraform -> app-skeleton; Tenant boot, Audit chain -> background-job; Customer/Property/Job/Inventory/Invoice -> crud-feature; ConnectRPC contracts -> app-skeleton; Zitadel/OpenFGA -> integration-http.
3. /decompose pours these. RESULT: 7 of 21 features fail to pour because the generic formula vars cannot express a Rust stack:
   - app-skeleton requires package_manager in {npm,pnpm,uv,poetry,auto}; plan supplied cargo / terraform / n-a -> off-enum, pour aborts (Repo skeleton, Closed grammar, OpenTelemetry, Terraform).
   - background-job requires trigger_type in {schedule,queue}; plan supplied internal / cron -> off-enum (Tenant boot, Audit chain).
   - migration requires down_outline; plan omitted it (Universal schema migration).
4. The 14 that DID pour carry the same root cause into their beads: crud-feature is REST-shaped, so Customer/Property/etc. beads specify REST verbs + HTTP status codes, contradicting the locked gRPC/tonic stack (see quality penalties on smbuild-mol-3ww and siblings). Zitadel/OpenFGA used integration-http even though the plan itself annotated them [needs-formula: oidc-client-rust] and [needs-formula: openfga-model].

EXPECTED: for a Rust-locked stack, /vision should select the stack-native variants that already exist in the library: app-skeleton-rust-cargo, crud-feature-rust, tenant-boot-rust, audit-chain-rust, otel-bootstrap-rust, oidc-client-rust, openfga-model, composer-grammar-version, grpc-tonic-service, terraform-aws-baseline. Those native formulas declare Rust-appropriate vars (no package_manager/trigger_type enum mismatch) and emit gRPC, not REST.

ACTUAL: /vision defaulted to the generic JS/Python/REST formulas. This was invisible in run 1 because pour agents silently improvised vars and the quality phase was a no-op; run-1 fixes (autonomous-build-l6g, -49u) exposed it.

This is distinct from autonomous-build-bje (which validates a var NAME against a chosen formula). Here the wrong FORMULA was chosen in the first place.
acceptance_criteria: On a Rust-locked stack, /vision selects the -rust / native formula variants (app-skeleton-rust-cargo, crud-feature-rust, tenant-boot-rust, audit-chain-rust, otel-bootstrap-rust, oidc-client-rust, openfga-model, composer-grammar-version, grpc-tonic-service, terraform-aws-baseline) rather than the generic ones; a fresh /decompose then pours every such feature with zero off-enum package_manager/trigger_type failures and emits gRPC (not REST) CRUD beads.
close_reason: vision now prefers stack-native formula variants (DEFAULT_STACK map + step-6 rule + off-enum tell); runtime half of AC (fresh /decompose pours clean) validates on next Rust-app build

title: cross-dep wiring applied 0 of 28 edges, including edges between two successfully-poured features
description: REPRO (smbuild /decompose run 2, 2026-05-28): decomposeReport.md reports 'Cross-feature deps applied: 0' and the fidelity verifier found only intra-molecule edges. Some missing edges touch the 7 unpoured features (expected to fail), BUT several declared edges are between TWO features that both poured successfully and still were not wired, e.g. Customer CRUD (smbuild-mol-cci) <- ConnectRPC/gRPC contracts v1 (smbuild-mol-jlk); Property CRUD <- Customer CRUD; Job <- Customer/Property. Those should have been wireable. This suggests the Phase-3 cross-dep wiring still under-applies (feature-name -> molecule-epic-id resolution failing, or the wiring aborting when ANY endpoint is unresolvable). NOTE: autonomous-build-zip (verify edges landed) is closed; this indicates that fix is incomplete or a separate resolution bug remains. Needs confirmation by the maintainer. title: Provide a cross-platform installer (install.sh); install.ps1 is Windows-only
description: Source: smbuild /decompose run 2026-05-28. Verification: confirmed bd search paths are .beads/formulas, ~/.beads/formulas, GT_ROOT/.beads/formulas, and none pointed at autonomous-build/formulas.

title: decompose quality phase scored zero beads (silent no-op, gate passes vacuously)
description: Source: smbuild /decompose run 2026-05-28. Evidence: log line 'Quality scoring done: 0 epics scored, 0 beads below 95' despite 18 successful pours and ~111 child beads. Phase 5 epic-discovery returned no epics with open non-epic children, so the per-epic scoring fanned out to nothing and the all-beads-at-95 verdict condition passed vacuously - no bead was actually scored against the buildability rubric. Likely the parent relationship is app-epic to molecule-root-epic to children, and epic-discovery did not traverse to the molecule-root epics. Verification: workflow logs plus live bd list showing 21 open epics and non-epic children present.
acceptance_criteria: workflows/decompose.js Phase 5 epic-discovery enumerates molecule-root epics (children of the app epic) so a non-empty pour set yields at least one epic and at least one scored bead; synthesis treats an empty quality-score set as NEEDS-FIX rather than a vacuous pass.

Synthesis adds qualityVacuous/qualityUndercovered guards: a zero-scored or under-scored run forces NEEDS-FIX instead of vacuously passing every(). Spec Phase 5 + Phase 8 updated.

title: build-batch.js uses Date.now() (lines 40, 643) — forbidden by Workflow runtime, /build-batch fails at launch (0 agents)
description: Upstream-promoted from smbuild BUILD-NOW run (2026-05-30, coevo tick 18). /build-batch failed instantly (25ms, 0 agents) with: 'Date.now() / new Date() are unavailable in workflow scripts (breaks resume)'. Root cause: workflows/build-batch.js declares const batchStart = Date.now() (L40) and durationMs = Date.now() - batchStart (L643), used only to print a cosmetic 'Duration:' line in the BATCH COMPLETE summary (L644-650). This blocks the entire authorized BUILD-NOW drain of smbuild's 75 ready beads. Same defect CLASS as the decompose.js fixes (forbidden runtime construct blocking a whole stage). Mechanical, safe-class.
design: Remove both Date.now() calls. The duration is purely cosmetic. Simplest correct fix: delete 'const batchStart = Date.now();' (L40) and the 'const durationMs = Date.now() - batchStart;' + durationStr lines (L643-644), and drop the 'Duration:' log line (L650). Keep the Merged/Blocked/Failed log lines. The Workflow runtime forbids Date.now()/new Date() because they break resume; do NOT substitute another wall-clock source — there is no allowed one. If a duration is genuinely wanted later, it must be stamped by the caller after the workflow returns (out of scope here).
acceptance_criteria: 1. grep -nE 'Date\.now\(\)|new Date\(' workflows/build-batch.js returns NO matches. 2. The BATCH COMPLETE summary still logs the substantive counts (Merged / Blocked / Failed). 3. node --check workflows/build-batch.js passes. 4. The 'one entry per phase() call' meta block and phase titles are unchanged (only timing code removed).
close_reason: Removed Date.now() from build-batch.js L40/L643; gate PASSED; commit 91c6774. AC1-3 verified.

title: /build-next: support meta-work mode (no Jankurai when no AGENTS.md)
description: /build-next assumes the repo is a Jankurai-tracked app scaffolded by /compose (has AGENTS.md, agent/baselines/, target/jankurai/ gitignored). When run on autonomous-build itself (meta-work editing skills/ and formulas/), none of that exists — Step 6 (jankurai kickoff) would refuse, Step 8's jankurai pass in post-build-gate is irrelevant. WHAT TO DO: add a meta-mode detection at the top of skills/build-next/SKILL.md — if AGENTS.md is absent at repo root, switch to meta-mode: (a) skip Step 5 worktree creation OR keep it (decide based on whether bd worktree still makes sense for skill/formula edits — probably yes for isolation), (b) skip Step 6 jankurai kickoff entirely, (c) in Step 8 run only the non-jankurai parts of post-build-gate.ps1, (d) commit + close steps unchanged. description: The per-worker quality gate runs in the worker's isolated worktree. That catches isolated regressions but not "passed in isolation, broken when combined with sibling bead." Add a post-merge gate step inside the orchestrator's serialized merge: after `git merge --no-ff bead/$id` lands on main, re-run hooks/post-build-gate.ps1 on main. If it fails, `git reset --hard ORIG_HEAD` to undo the merge, mark the bead blocked with the failure summary, do NOT close the bead. This is the safety net for the (currently) no-testPlanFile world.

title: Add freshness check to /build-next before claiming a bead
description: Borrowed from refs/epic-quality-pass §Freshness check. A bead is a snapshot of the codebase at filing time. Other beads (or human work) may have landed since and silently satisfied this bead's AC. Today /build-next just claims the next ready bead and dispatches a builder — even if the work is already done. WHAT TO DO: in skills/build-next/SKILL.md, before calling bd update --status=in_progress, run a quick freshness check on the bead's load-bearing claims. If the bead says a file should not exist, glob to confirm. If it asserts wiring is missing, grep to confirm the wiring still isn't there. If it cites file sizes or line counts, spot-check. Three outcomes: (a) fresh → proceed normally; (b) drifted (details moved but gap still real) → proceed but record a 'freshness note' in the builder's prompt; (c) stale (AC already met) → close the bead with reason 'AC already met by <ref>' and move to the next ready bead. Keep the check fast — a few grep/glob calls, not a deep code review.

title: vision-eval: scale harness for plan coverage determinism
description:
A scale harness that turns "are our plans deterministic / in line with expectations?" from a vibe into a dashboard with numbers. Tests /vision output (plan.lock.json concerns[]/coverage[], plan.md, tenets.md) against hand-authored expectation manifests, across a fixture corpus, over K runs each. Proposed in the 2026-05-28 design session.

WHY now possible: the coverage gate (epic autonomous-build-bfo) makes plan coverage MACHINE-CHECKABLE (concerns[], coverage[], decidedness, incomplete). The eval checks the CONTRACT, not the prose.

LAYERS:
- L1 contract validation (free, deterministic): schema v2 valid; every concern decided; every must-have in coverage[]; incomplete iff blocking questions.
- L2 expectation assertions: per fixture, assert output vs a hand-authored manifest (required concerns -> addressed; excluded -> excluded-with-reason; adversarial fixtures -> BLOCK).
- L3 stability (the determinism metric): K runs/fixture; coverage-stability (same (applicability,status) across runs, target ~100%) + verdict-stability (block/pass, must be 100%); content variance (data-model/feature count) MEASURED, not gated.
- L4 evidence-quality judge (LLM, periodic): adversarial panel scores each addressed-evidence -> vagueness rate (the rubber-stamp failure mode).
- L5 downstream propagation (expensive, small sample): a few BLESSED plans through decompose/build-batch; did bfo.9 catch lying concerns? did anything leak past the plan gate?

HARNESS: a vision-eval dynamic workflow (enumerate corpus -> pipeline fixture x K runs -> mechanical L1-L3 in pure JS -> L4 judge fan-out -> scorecard + baseline delta). Scorecard ratcheted like jankurai witness; CI-fail on regression. Cost discipline: L1-L3 every commit; L4-L5 periodic.

CLOSING LOOP: the corpus axes (CLI/web/pipeline/PII/multi-tenant/public/scale) ARE the candidate per-archetype profiles deferred from the design session. Consistent override patterns in the corpus = the empirical signal that a profile has earned its existence.

DEPENDS ON the coverage gate (autonomous-build-bfo); most powerful after the vision workflow (autonomous-build-ih5).

title: vision-eval: fixture corpus + expectation manifests (the oracle)
description:
Build the fixture corpus + expectation manifests - the oracle the whole eval grades against.

WHY: the manifests encode "what we expect"; a sloppy oracle makes the eval meaningless. This is the main human-authored investment.

ACCEPTANCE:
- A directory of >=8 vision.md fixtures spanning the coverage-relevant axes: greenfield multi-tenant web; single-user offline CLI; data pipeline; public unauthenticated API; app with an S6 privacy constraint; app with an S8 scale/latency target; app with external integrations; plus adversarial cases (must-have excluded by a non-goal; must-have with no matching formula; empty product sections).
- Each fixture paired with a machine-readable expectation manifest asserting: which concerns should be required/optional/excluded, which must-haves should map to coverage, and whether the run should BLOCK (incomplete:true).
- Manifests reference the concernIds pinned in docs/PLAN_CONCERNS.md.
- A README documents the corpus axes and how each axis maps to the deferred per-archetype profiles.

description:
vision-eval workflow: L4 evidence-quality judge fan-out (vagueness rate).

WHY: the one thing not mechanically checkable - is the evidence falsifiable, or rubber-stamped vagueness? This is the direct measure of the failure mode the whole coverage design fears.

ACCEPTANCE:
- For each addressed concern across all runs, an adversarial judge panel (2-3 agents, majority) scores whether the evidence is FALSIFIABLE (cites a feature/formula/tenet/gate/stack-pin) or a bare assertion.
- Emits a vagueness rate (fraction of addressed concerns whose evidence fails the falsifiability bar).
- Judge prompts are self-contained (run in app cwd) and embed the docs/PLAN_CONCERNS.md "Evidence - what counts" bar inline.

title: Add testPlan{File,Cases,Coverage} fields to formula schema and existing formulas
description: Borrowed from refs/design-to-beads §Test Architecture. Every formula step today implies tests (endpoint-tests, ui-tests) but writes no per-bead test metadata. /build-next has no signal for where tests live, so each bead invents a new test file — at scale this fragments coverage. WHAT TO DO: (1) extend formula step schema with optional testPlanFile (string path), testPlanCases (int), testPlanCoverage (semicolon-separated string). (2) extend bd cook / bd mol pour so these flow into bd metadata at pour time. (3) retrofit formulas/crud-feature.formula.yaml and formulas/app-skeleton.formula.yaml — for each test-flavored step, set the three fields with sensible defaults parameterized by the step's variables. (4) document in formulas/README.md. This unblocks the quality-pass skill (sibling bead) which will assert metadata.testPlanFile is populated.
notes:
FOUNDATIONAL BLOCKER DISCOVERED 2026-05-28:

While starting work on testPlan{File,Cases,Coverage} fields, I verified the existing formulas don't actually load into bd 0.55.3.

What I tested:
- `bd formula list` from autonomous-build repo → "No formulas found" despite .formula.yaml files being in the search path.
- Copied crud-feature.formula.yaml to a fresh bd-init'd project's .beads/formulas/ → still "No formulas found".
- bd formula convert only handles JSON → TOML; no YAML support.
- Wrote a tiny JSON test formula → bd parsed it but dropped the `name:` field (canonical field is `formula = "..."`), dropped `variables`, dropped `acceptance` per step.

Comparing autonomous-build YAML vs bd's canonical schema (see beads-release.formula.toml):
- `name:` should be `formula = "..."` (top-level)
- `variables: [...]` should be `[vars.<name>]` tables
- `depends_on:` per step should be `needs:`
- `acceptance:` per step has no bd equivalent — must be embedded in description
- `priority:` and `labels:` per step are not in the canonical example
- bd requires top-level `type = "workflow"` and `version = 1`

Implication: the entire /compose pipeline is non-functional against bd 0.55.3 today. `bd cook crud-feature` fails because the formula isn't recognized. Adding testPlan fields to YAML formulas bd can't load is wasted motion.

Right sequence:
1. Convert formulas to bd's canonical TOML schema (foundational — new bead)
2. Validate the converted formulas end-to-end with bd cook + bd mol pour against a scratch project (foundational — new bead)
3. THEN add testPlan fields to the working TOML formulas (this bead, autonomous-build-a2m)
4. Update /compose to write the testPlan blocks as bd metadata after pour

Blocking this bead until 1 and 2 are filed and closed.

title: build-batch prep: bd worktree create exits nonzero despite success + branch-name mismatch -> all beads wrongly blocked
description: Repro (smbuild 2026-05-30 build-now run): /build-batch dispatched 8 waves, 16 beads, 0 merged, ALL blocked with note 'worktree create failed'. Root cause: the prep agent (workflows/build-batch.js ~L428) runs `bd worktree create "task-<id>" --json` and blocks the bead 'if it errors'. But bd 0.55.4's worktree create ALWAYS exits nonzero ('Error: failed to create worktree: exit status 128 ... already exists') EVEN WHEN it fully creates a usable worktree (dir + branch + synced issues.jsonl; verified bd ready/show work inside). So every bead is misclassified as failed. SECOND bug: prep passes no --branch, so bd defaults branch=name=task-<id>, but prep's return + crash-recovery (L198) + merge (L576) all assume branch 'bead/<id>' -> merge would also fail. FIX (Option 2, verified): prep step 2 should (a) pre-clean stale task-<id> worktree + bead/<id> branch, (b) run `bd worktree create "task-<id>" --branch "bead/<id>"` ignoring exit code, (c) VERIFY via git worktree list --porcelain that worktree <repo>/task-<id> with branch refs/heads/bead/<id> exists + dir has files, treat exists=success, (d) only block if verification truly fails. Keep build-batch.js + build-batch.spec.md in sync (T3). Acceptance: prep no longer blocks on a successful-but-nonzero bd worktree create; branch is bead/<id> end-to-end; a /build-batch drain on smbuild merges beads instead of 0/all-blocked.

title: plan-stage coverage gate: must-haves + concerns
description:
Guarantee every vision.md must-have and every success-metric step makes it into plan.lock.json, with a falsifiable coverage map that blocks /decompose when something is dropped. Closes the gap where /vision can silently drop a must-have during freeform feature decomposition: every downstream gate validates against the plan, never against the vision, so a dropped must-have is invisible until ship. MVP = bfo.1-4. Follow-up = bfo.5.

EXPANDED 2026-05-28 (design session): folds in cross-cutting CONCERN coverage (data-model, authn, authz, secrets, data-lifecycle, error-handling, observability, external-integrations, perf-envelope, abuse-surface) on the SAME mechanism. A concern is forced to a decided state - addressed (with falsifiable evidence) or excluded (with reason) - never silently omitted. This attacks OMISSION (invisible) where the must-have track attacks dropped product features.

Concern track: vocabulary (bfo.6) -> schema concerns[] (folded into bfo.1) -> vision emit (bfo.7) -> decidedness + required-excluded contradiction gate (bfo.8) -> decompose truthfulness verify (bfo.9). The decidedness gate reuses the same incomplete:true -> /decompose-refusal plumbing as the must-have gate (bfo.4); no new downstream gate for the MVP. Only the truthfulness check (bfo.9, "addressed" actually traces to a bead) is genuinely new decompose-side work, and it is follow-up grade like bfo.5.

The larger /vision -> dynamic-workflow conversion is a SEPARATE epic that DEPENDS on this contract. Land this against the current skill first; do not couple coverage value to the rewrite.
close_reason: All 10 children closed. Plan-stage coverage gate is live end-to-end: schema v2 records mustHaves/successMetric/coverage/concerns (bfo.1); /decompose accepts v2 (bfo.2); /vision emits + gates the coverage map and concern decidedness, blocking /decompose on a dropped must-have or undecided/contradicted concern (bfo.3/4/5/7/8); /decompose verifies addressed concerns trace to a real bead (bfo.9) and pours gated enforcement beads for non-feature concerns/NFRs (bfo.10, unified with the lbq NFR track). Note: bfo.4/6.6 etc. live in skills/vision/SKILL.md and the decompose Phase 3.5/6 — the larger /vision->workflow conversion (ih5) and vision-eval harness (4vj) remain as separate dependent epics.

title: plan.lock schema v2: add mustHaves[], successMetric, coverage[], concerns[]
description:

title: decompose Phase 6.C (verifier-C + reconcile) yields a FALSE musthave-gap when a must-have's covering feature poured but its child beads are implementation-named, not must-have-named
description:
## Summary
Phase 6.C verifier C (`workflows/decompose.js:790-825`) marks each THIS-phase must-have "covered" iff >=1 open bead implements a covering feature, else "gap", with an explicit "be conservative: a must-have you cannot confidently map to a bead is a gap" (L815). Verifier C is **independent — blind to verifier A's plan->dag (feature->pour-root) map** (L817). The reconcile step takes `mustHaveGap = (verifierC.traceable === 'gap')` at face value (L841), with no cross-check against A's feature->pour-root map. Result: when a covering feature DID pour an epic whose children are titled by implementation detail (not by the must-have's wording), verifier C string-matches must-have->bead, fails to credit those children, and conservatively flags "gap" -> a hard `musthave-gap` NEEDS-FIX on work that demonstrably exists.

## Evidence (smbuild, 2026-05-29 real pour — 209 beads, honest NEEDS-FIX)
- M9 must-have text: *"Tamper-evident audit chain. Sensitive actions (e.g., invoice sent) append a hash-linked audit record."*
- Covering feature "Tamper-evident audit chain (smbuild-audit blessed writer + Object-Lock anchor)" POURED epic `smbuild-mol-0zm`, whose description is a near-verbatim M9 implementation ("the smbuild-audit crate as the only blessed writer, the audit_chain Postgres table with RLS + hash-verify trigger, ... a daily witness job anchoring chain heads to S3 Object Lock").
- Children scored 100/100: `smbuild-mol-dp3` (audit_chain table + RLS + trigger), `-77q` (tamper detection trigger), `-6h5` (append), `-cph` (witness), `-d72` (cargo-deny audit_chain ban).
- Phase 6.A (verifier A) CORRECTLY connected the feature -> epic (report line 21: `... -> smbuild-mol-0zm ✓`).
- Yet Phase 6.C matrix recorded `M9 | GAP | Tamper-evident audit chain | (none)` -> `mustHaveGap=true` -> dominant blocking NEEDS-FIX cause, on a DAG that is otherwise coverage- and traceability-clean (48/48 beads traced).

## Root cause
Per the spec's own logic (L813: "covered iff >=1 bead implements a covering feature") M9 SHOULD be covered — the bug is verifier-C execution: under independence + conservatism it does not follow covering-feature -> pour-root -> children, and reconcile does not reconcile A vs C.

## Fix direction (one of)
1. In reconcile: when verifier C flags a THIS-phase must-have gap whose covering feature DID pour a root per verifier A / Phase 6.A, credit that root's children and downgrade to "covered" (or surface as a verifier-disagreement-needs-review advisory, not a hard NEEDS-FIX).
2. Give verifier C the feature->pour-root map so it can follow feature -> epic -> children instead of independently string-matching must-have -> bead.
Keep adversarial independence for the plan<->dag direction; this is specifically the must-have->bead inheritance that A already computed.

Surfaced by the smbuild plan-loop dogfood run on 2026-05-29. Sibling finding: autonomous-build-3ch (args footgun on the same run).
acceptance_criteria: A THIS-phase must-have whose covering feature poured a real epic with implementation-named children is reconciled to status='covered' (beads credited), not 'gap'. Concretely: smbuild M9 (tamper-evident audit chain), covered by epic smbuild-mol-0zm + children (audit_chain table+RLS+trigger, tamper-detection trigger, append, witness), is 'covered' — not a musthave-gap NEEDS-FIX.

title: No first-class product-phase/milestone: cannot decompose/build a plan one phase at a time
description: Upstream capture from smbuild (coevo, 2026-05-29). Category: enhancement. Observation: the pipeline has no product-phasing primitive. plan.lock.json has no phase/milestone/tier field; /decompose pours ALL must-have features (featureOrder from SS3); /build-batch drains ALL ready non-epic beads with no --phase/--label/--epic/--milestone subset filter. Today phasing is expressible ONLY via the SS3-must-have vs SS4-nice-to-have boundary (nice-to-haves are recorded but not poured), or via app-level prose ('Phases per tenets SS1' in the old smbuild plan) that nothing enforces. PROPOSED: optional phase/milestone tier on must-haves -> plan.lock, plus a --phase/--milestone scope on /decompose and /build-batch so a multi-phase plan builds incrementally (v1 now, v2 later) instead of all-or-nothing. SURFACED BY: deferring smbuild's NL composer (M1) to v2 — works today only by moving it to SS4, which means it is never poured at all rather than 'poured into a later phase'. LOW-MED priority enhancement.
acceptance_criteria:
A product-phasing primitive is plumbed through the three artifacts named in the proposal, verifiable by grep/file-exists on autonomous-build:

1. SCHEMA — `schemas/plan.lock.schema.json` defines an optional phase/milestone field on `featureOrder[]` items (and/or on `mustHaves[]`): `grep -E '"(phase|milestone|tier)"' schemas/plan.lock.schema.json` returns a match, and `docs/PLAN_LOCK.md` documents it (a "### `phase`" / milestone field-reference heading is present).

2. /decompose — `workflows/decompose.spec.md` documents a `--phase`/`--milestone` (or `--label`/`--epic`) scope option that restricts which `featureOrder[]` entries are poured: a new row in the Inputs/flags table (the `| \`--phase` table line) AND the Phase-3 pour text reference the field. The companion `workflows/decompose.js` references the flag/field name (`grep -nE 'phase|milestone' workflows/decompose.js` matches the new option).

3. /build-batch — `workflows/build-batch.spec.md` documents a `--phase`/`--milestone`/`--label`/`--epic` subset filter applied to the `bd ready` candidate set in Phase 2.1, and `workflows/build-batch.js` references it, so a single phase builds incrementally instead of draining ALL ready non-epic beads (`grep -nE 'phase|milestone|--label|--epic' workflows/build-batch.spec.md workflows/build-batch.js` matches the new filter, not just existing internal "Phase N" headings).

Each spec change is co-committed with its `.js` script (per CLAUDE.md kept-in-sync rule), and the new option is exercised by a workflow test under `workflows/test/`. Acceptance passes when all three greps match and the decompose/build-batch specs and their scripts agree on the option name.

title: vision gate 8.6 musthave-nongoal: substring check false-positives on a NEGATED must-have
description: Upstream capture from smbuild (coevo plan-loop tick 4, 2026-05-29). Category: gate/vision. CONFIRMED-PLAUSIBLE (read the code). musthaveNongoalConflicts() in workflows/vision.js (~L607-624) strips a LEADING negation from the SS5 non-goal (L613) but applies NO negation-awareness to the SS3 must-have: it flags any must-have whose text .includes(phrase). smbuild M1='Natural-language intake -> closed-grammar composition ... no free-form code generation'; SS5 non-goal='Free-form / arbitrary code generation — closed-grammar only'. The '/' split yields phrase 'free-form'; M1 contains 'free-form' INSIDE its negation 'no free-form', so gate 8.6 fires musthave-nongoal-contradiction though M1 and SS5 AGREE. Produced blocking openQuestion #6 on a coherent vision. PROPOSED FIX: apply the same negation strip to the must-have side that L613 applies to the non-goal — skip a match when the phrase is immediately preceded by no/not/never/without/avoid in the must-have; and/or reject 9-char fragments like 'free-form'. Evidence: workflows/vision.js L603-624; smbuild plan.lock.json openQuestions; coevo journal tick 4. NOTE: vision.js is under active ih5 migration — coordinate (meta-quiescence) before editing.
acceptance_criteria:
FIX `musthaveNongoalConflicts()` in `/home/cstaulbee/.openclaw/workspace/autonomous-build/workflows/vision.js` (~L607-627) so a must-have that NEGATES a forbidden phrase no longer false-positives as a must-have/non-goal contradiction (gate 8.6).

Concrete, self-verifiable acceptance:

1. Code presence: In the must-have match loop (currently L616-622), the `m.text.toLowerCase().includes(phrase)` check (L618) must be guarded by the same negation-awareness already applied to the non-goal side at L613. Specifically: a match must be SKIPPED when the occurrence of `phrase` inside the must-have text is immediately preceded by a negator (no/not/never/without/don't/avoid/exclude(s/d)). Verify: `grep -n` in `workflows/vision.js` shows, inside `musthaveNongoalConflicts`, a regex/check applying the negator token set (`/\b(?:no|not|never|without|don'?t|avoid|exclude[ds]?)\b/`) to the must-have text around the phrase position — not only to the non-goal at L613.

2. Behavioral / gate-pass: A unit-level or gate-level check proving the regression case no longer fires. Given mustHaves = [{ id: "M1", text: "Natural-language intake -> closed-grammar composition ... no free-form code generation" }] and nonGoals = ["Free-form / arbitrary code generation — closed-grammar only"], `musthaveNongoalConflicts(mustHaves, nonGoals)` MUST return an empty array (no conflict). Equivalently, a test asserting `out.length === 0` for this fixture passes, while a genuine contradiction (must-have text "Generate free-form code at runtime" vs the same non-goal) still returns one conflict — confirming the fix narrows false positives without disabling real detection.

title: Loop blocked-detection uses 'bd blocked' (dependency-only) — misses status=blocked beads the loop itself sets
description:
CONFIRMED 2026-05-29 while draining this repo's backlog. 'bd blocked' surfaces only DEPENDENCY-blocked beads (those with an unmet open blocker); it does NOT surface beads whose status FIELD is 'blocked' with no open dependency. Repro: autonomous-build-mvh.1.3 and mvh.2.3 have status=blocked, deps:[] — 'bd blocked' prints 'No blocked issues', while 'bd list --status blocked' shows both.

WHY LOAD-BEARING: the loop's escalation mechanism IS the status field — /build-next Step 8 does 'bd update <id> --status=blocked', /build-batch marks failed/blocked beads via status, /escalate is meant to summarize them. But the drain-to-blocked + escalate detection across the pipeline reads 'bd blocked':
  - skills/build-next/SKILL.md:62  ('Check bd blocked --json. If any -> /escalate')
  - skills/escalate/SKILL.md:14    ('bd blocked --json')
  - skills/orchestrate/SKILL.md:42 ('bd blocked --json')
  - workflows/build-batch.js:108/663/667 (blocked/failed beads 'appear in bd blocked')
  - workflows/retro.js:319         (gathers blocked via 'bd blocked')
So a drain-to-blocked where every remaining bead is status=blocked (the normal escalation output) reads as ZERO blocked -> the loop takes the DONE/retro path or reports nothing-to-escalate instead of paging the human. The two smoke-test beads here are exactly this case: invisible to 'bd blocked', so /escalate would summarize nothing.

FIX (recommend): detect human-needed blocks via 'bd list --status blocked --json' (the explicit, human-actionable set), NOT 'bd blocked' (dependency-blocked, which usually auto-resolves when the blocker closes and is rarely escalation-worthy). Decide per consumer whether to escalate on status=blocked only, or the union of both. Update all 5 consumers consistently. Build-batch's '--status=blocked' marking + its own 'appear in bd blocked' assumption must be reconciled (its comment is currently wrong).

ALSO: 'bd stats' Blocked count disagreed too (showed 1, then 0, while 2 status-blocked beads existed) — verify whether stats counts dependency-blocked or status-blocked, and whether concurrent-session .beads staleness contributes. Note: this host runs parallel claude sessions on a shared checkout.
acceptance_criteria: All five consumers (skills/build-next, skills/escalate, skills/orchestrate, workflows/build-batch.js, workflows/retro.js) detect human-needed blocked work via the status field ('bd list --status blocked --json'), not 'bd blocked' alone. Verify: (1) grep shows no consumer relies solely on 'bd blocked' for drain-to-blocked/escalation; (2) a bead set to --status=blocked with deps:[] is surfaced by the new detection (manual repro: mvh.1.3/mvh.2.3 must be reported); (3) build-batch.js's stale 'appear in bd blocked' comment is corrected to match.

title: Tier the Jankurai gate: ratchet-on-commit, fix witness deadlock
description:
Make Jankurai actually blocking during the unattended build window via a regression-only ratchet, and fix the latent deadlock that lbq.14 introduced. Full spec + 2 review rounds + locked decisions in docs/JANKURAI_GATING_PROPOSAL.md.

PROBLEM: lbq.14 auto-stamps a ~44 scaffold score as the witness baseline and the gate hard-fails on 'jankurai witness' exit code. Review proved witness BAKES IN the absolute 85 floor (blocks an identical zero-delta tree), so the next real app build deadlocks on its first commits (ready>0, no human-clearable cause). Latent only because this meta-repo is never Jankurai-governed.

DECISIONS (made on owner's behalf): D1 ratchet-only in v1, no absolute floor anywhere; D2 baseline acceptance rides the human BLESSED gate, auto-accept-with-loud-note on the --auto-bless walk-away path; D3 parse the audit receipt's decision.ratchet ourselves now, file 'witness --mode ratchet' upstream as non-blocking cleanup.

MECHANISM (verified vs jankurai 1.5.1): parse audit-fast.json decision.ratchet; BLOCK iff score_delta < -TOLERANCE OR len(new_hard_findings)>0 OR len(new_caps)>0. Never gate on decision.passed/ratchet.passed (floor-contaminated). See doc for the 9 load-bearing rules.

title: Replace witness-exit-code enforcement with receipt-parse ratchet in both gate ports
description:
Supersedes lbq.14. The current gate hard-fails on 'jankurai witness' exit code, which bakes in the absolute 85 floor and deadlocks every sub-85 app. Replace it with a regression-only ratchet parsed from the audit receipt.

In hooks/post-build-gate.sh AND .ps1 (keep both ports behaviourally identical), after the advisory 'jankurai audit . --changed-fast --baseline <blessed> --json target/jankurai/audit-fast.json' run, parse audit-fast.json -> decision.ratchet and:
  BLOCK iff  score_delta < -TOLERANCE (TOLERANCE=2; allowed_drop is pinned at 0 by the CLI, apply tolerance in our own compare)
          OR len(new_hard_findings) > 0
          OR len(new_caps) > 0
Stop calling 'jankurai witness' for enforcement.

LOAD-BEARING RULES (from review, see docs/JANKURAI_GATING_PROPOSAL.md):
1. Parse the three fields, NEVER decision.passed / ratchet.passed (floor-contaminated -> reintroduces deadlock).
2. A real --baseline must be passed (else self-references: baseline_score==score, deltas always 0, inert no-op).
3. Parse the AUDIT receipt (audit-fast.json), not the witness receipt (field names differ: new_caps/new_hard_findings vs caps_added/new_findings).
4. --changed-fast audit always exits 0 -> decision must come from the parsed receipt, never the exit code.
5. Crash-vs-block: BLOCK only when receipt exists, parses as JSON, and has decision.ratchet with numeric fields; else SKIP-with-loud-warning. Assert schema_version; treat skew as SKIP not block.
8. Base ref: pin --changed-from to a resolvable LOCAL ref (merge-base with pre-build HEAD, or baseline commit sha) — never origin/main (absent in never-pushed repo -> fails open).
9. Do not let --policy smuggle the 85 floor back: BLOCK decision reads only the three ratchet fields, ignoring the floor verdict.
acceptance_criteria: Both gate ports parse audit-fast.json decision.ratchet and block only on score_delta<-TOLERANCE / new_hard_findings / new_caps; no call to 'jankurai witness' for enforcement; a sub-85 but non-regressing commit PASSES; invalid/missing receipt -> loud SKIP not block; --changed-from uses a local ref; both ports verified behaviourally identical.
close_reason: both gate ports parse audit-fast.json decision.ratchet (score_delta<-2 / new_hard_findings / new_caps), never decision.passed/ratchet.passed; witness no longer called for enforcement; --baseline passed when present, --changed-from is local HEAD; invalid/missing/schema-skew receipt -> loud SKIP; sub-85 non-regressing PASSES (unit-verified); ports mirrored (.ps1 unrunnable here, no pwsh). Live BLOCK path validates on first real app build with a baseline (igu.2).

title: Decompose: move baseline acceptance after BLESSED + autoChain auto-accept + high-water-mark re-stamp
description:
Fix the baseline-trust and staleness problems (D2 + rule 7). Touches workflows/decompose.js and decompose.spec.md.

D2 — baseline trust: today lbq.14 unconditionally auto-stamps the scaffold score as the witness baseline in Phase 2 BEFORE the verdict. Move acceptance to AFTER the BLESSED verdict on the attended path so the human blesses the starting floor (README contract: baselines are 'tracked, TRUSTED'). On the --auto-bless / autoChain walk-away path (/orchestrate, where no human reads decomposeReport.md), auto-accept the scaffold score as baseline AND write a conspicuous 'trusted-by-policy, not by human' note in the commit + report. A never-accepted baseline means a ratchet that never fires — reopening the gap in the exact unattended window. Plumb the autoChain/--auto-bless signal into the baseline step.

RULE 7 — advancing baseline / high-water mark: a baseline frozen at decompose-time goes stale (after the app climbs to 80, a regression 80->70 still reads +26 vs the frozen 44 and the ratchet dies). On a GREEN commit where the new whole-repo score exceeds baseline_score, the gate re-stamps the baseline UPWARD only (never lowers it). Monotonic: human blessed the starting line, machine only ratchets it up; bounds cumulative drift to TOLERANCE against the high-water mark. Decide which port owns the re-stamp (gate, post-green, app-mode only) and the commit timing.

Also: .gitignore care so tracked agent/ files (baseline + audit-policy.toml) are not swallowed while target/jankurai/ stays ignored. Write agent/audit-policy.toml from 'jankurai govern' for tracking (rule 9: its 85 floor must not block).
acceptance_criteria: Attended path: baseline accepted after BLESSED verdict, not before. Walk-away (--auto-bless) path: scaffold baseline auto-accepted with a loud recorded note. Green commits re-stamp the baseline upward only (never down). agent/ baseline + policy are tracked; target/jankurai stays ignored.
close_reason: D2: Phase 2 no longer auto-stamps baseline pre-verdict (superseded lbq.14); new Phase 8 accept-baseline agent accepts the scaffold whole-repo audit only on BLESSED — attended commit vs loud [TRUSTED-BY-POLICY, NOT BY HUMAN] auto-accept on autoChain/--auto-bless; writes agent/audit-policy.toml from jankurai govern (rule 9 tracking-only), .gitignore care (agent/ tracked, target/jankurai/ ignored). Rule 7: gate re-stamps baseline UPWARD-only on green via fresh whole-repo audit, best-effort, GATE_RESTAMP=off for build-batch parallel workers (serial post-merge gate on main + build-next own the advance). Both gate ports mirrored. Verified: comparator exit codes, e2e .sh advance 5->35 + commit, GATE_RESTAMP=off and no-baseline skip paths, meta-mode gate still PASSES. decompose.spec.md kept in sync; build-next SKILL Step 8 refreshed off the superseded witness. Note: pwsh absent so .ps1 mirrored by hand.

title: Wire GATE_REQUIRE_BASELINE=1 from app-mode callers; loud-fail vs quiet-skip on missing baseline
description:
Rule 6 — distinguish 'app mode, baseline missing (decompose bug)' from 'meta mode, no baseline by design'. The gate hook can't tell modes apart by itself, but the callers already compute meta-vs-app.

In skills/build-next/SKILL.md (Step 8 gate invocation) and workflows/build-batch.js (merge gate + per-worker build invocation), pass GATE_REQUIRE_BASELINE=1 in app mode. In the gate ports, when GATE_REQUIRE_BASELINE=1 and no baseline exists -> LOUD FAIL (decompose bug). When the signal is unset (meta mode) and no baseline exists -> quiet SKIP, ratchet never fires (correct: Jankurai governs outputs, not this repo).
acceptance_criteria: App-mode build with no baseline hard-fails loudly with a clear 'no blessed baseline — decompose bug' message; meta-mode runs keep the quiet SKIP; signal passed from both build-next and build-batch.
close_reason: Implemented across two commits (d3b0e8b caller signal + 9897ad3 gate-port loud-fail & build-batch wiring). Gate ports (.sh/.ps1): no-baseline branch hard-fails when GATE_REQUIRE_BASELINE=1 (app mode, decompose bug), quiet SKIP when unset (meta). Signal set by build-next SKILL.md Step 8 (app mode only) and build-batch.js (per-worker + post-merge gate; always app mode). spec synced. Verified .sh: app/=1 no-baseline -> GATE FAILED exit 1, meta/unset -> exit 0; bash -n + node --check pass; .ps1 mirrors branch (pwsh absent here).

title: vision -> dynamic workflow (hybrid: thin skill shell + concern fan-out engine)
description:
Convert /vision from a turn-by-turn skill into a dynamic workflow (like /decompose and /build-batch), keeping a THIN /vision skill as the human-facing conversational shell. Decided in the 2026-05-28 design session.

SHAPE (hybrid): the thin skill does the product dialog (reads vision.md, quotes back non-goals/constraints/success-metric/must-haves, helps fill gaps), then invokes the vision workflow; the workflow is the deterministic engine. Skill warmth at the edges, deterministic engine in the middle.

WHY a workflow: per-concern purpose-built agents (1 per applicable concern) with structured outputs, a COMPUTED decidedness verdict (not vibes-in-chat), state isolation, and the adversarial-verification machinery - exactly the determinism properties the design session was chasing. A skill spawning agents ad hoc (as /vision's 3-agent off-stack consult already does) gives none of those guarantees. The human-in-loop tension is resolved by the thin shell + a NEEDS-INPUT return that maps to vision.md edits + re-run (a durable-input loop, more auditable than transient chat Q&A).

PHASE STRUCTURE:
1. intake/validate vision.md (sequential) -> NEEDS-INPUT blocking questions if incoherent/incomplete.
2. skeleton: data model + feature order + formula picks (sequential; these are coupled).
3. concern fan-out: 1 agent per applicable concern, each reading the FROZEN skeleton read-only, deciding only its concern, emitting {status, evidence|reason}. (Frozen-skeleton input is load-bearing: concerns are far more coupled than features, so fanning out from scratch yields N inconsistent mental models.)
4. reconcile: contradiction scan (required+excluded), assemble plan.lock v2 + tenets + plan.md, compute decidedness verdict -> COMPLETE | NEEDS-INPUT.

DEPENDS ON the coverage-gate contract (epic autonomous-build-bfo): PLAN_CONCERNS.md vocabulary, plan.lock v2 concerns[], and the derivation rules must exist first. This is the LATER, larger track. Children may be atomized further at build time.

title: vision-workflow: author workflows/vision.spec.md (hybrid model + 4 phases + schemas + sync rules)
description:
Author workflows/vision.spec.md - the maintainer source-of-truth spec for the vision workflow.

WHY hand-authored JS (like decompose, not first-run-generated): vision is as load-bearing as decompose - it is the SOURCE of the contract everything downstream consumes. A reviewed spec+js pair is more trustworthy than "whatever the model emitted once."

ACCEPTANCE:
- New file workflows/vision.spec.md.
- Documents the hybrid model (thin /vision skill shell + workflow engine) and why vision stays human-in-the-loop while the engine is deterministic.
- Specifies all 4 phases (intake, skeleton, concern fan-out, reconcile) with per-agent tools, inputs, outputs, and structured-output schemas.
- States the self-contained-prompt rule (agents run in app cwd; spec does not ship) and the concern-vocabulary-inlined-as-JS-const sync rule (PLAN_CONCERNS.md <-> vision.js, mirroring decompose.spec.md<->decompose.js).
- Specifies the COMPLETE | NEEDS-INPUT return contract and how NEEDS-INPUT maps to vision.md edits + re-run.
- Cross-references the bfo contract (concerns[], coverage[], PLAN_CONCERNS.md).

title: Triage drain (2026-05-29)
decompose.js write-report template has unescaped backticks (1185-1186) -> Workflow runtime parse error, /decompose cannot run
description:
## Summary
The /decompose write-report agent template literal in `workflows/decompose.js` contains UNESCAPED backticks at lines 1185-1186 (around `--auto-bless`), while every other inline-code reference in the same template is escaped (e.g. lines 1174, 1194 use \`...\`). Under the Workflow runtime's source transform these raw backticks break tokenization, throwing **"Assigning to rvalue (1084:33)"** — the workflow fails to load and `/decompose` cannot run AT ALL.

## Evidence
- `Workflow {scriptPath: workflows/decompose.js}` → parse error "Assigning to rvalue (1084:33)".
- `Workflow {name:"decompose"}` → not registered (only deep-research/build-batch/retro are).
- A throwaway copy with the 4 backticks escaped (`\`--auto-bless\``) PARSED and ran the full pipeline (32 agents, dry-run decompose of smbuild). `node --check` passes on BOTH copies (node's full-file tokenization differs from the Workflow runtime's transform — node-valid is a red herring; the runtime that actually runs /decompose rejects it).

## Fix
Escape the 4 raw backticks on decompose.js:1185 and :1186 to match the established style at :1174/:1194. One-line-class change. Then `node --check` + a `Workflow{scriptPath}` load must succeed.

## Provenance
Found by the smbuild plan-creation co-evolution run (coevo_smbuild --scope plan). Blocks reaching a real /decompose BLESSED. NOT the 3fr.1 path (/vision passed clean).
notes: FIX APPLIED (working tree, uncommitted): escaped the 4 backticks around `--auto-bless` in workflows/decompose.js lines 1206-1207 (matching the escaped style at L177/L1044). Verified: node --check OK; no raw backticks remain on 1206-1207. Integration test = re-decompose (decompose.js parses under Workflow runtime).
