title: Missing concern-enforcement + e2e formulas block decompose BLESSED (data-lifecycle, perf, abuse-surface, e2e-acceptance) -- design decision
description:
## Summary
The smbuild plan is well-formed (/vision: incomplete:false, 10/10 concerns decided, 9/9 must-haves; native Rust formulas bound — 3fr.1 NOT regressing), but /decompose Phase 3.5 finds NO installed enforcement formula for several concerns/NFRs, each of which forces NEEDS-FIX. These are genuinely-absent native variants → NEW formulas = design decisions. Per runbook: file + escalate, do NOT self-author overnight.

## Missing formulas (recommended names from the decompose report)
1. `concern-enforcement-data-lifecycle` — retention/residency test: soft-deleted rows hidden-but-recoverable, tenant purge fires after the 30-day offboarding window, Object-Lock-archived audit/PDF objects immutable before expiry.
2. `concern-enforcement-perf` — load/latency test driving tenant-provision → first-app-published, asserting successMetric S2 p50<=15min / p95<=30min; fail the gate when breached.
3. `concern-enforcement-abuse-surface` — input-validation + rate-limit test: malformed/oversized inputs rejected at the typed-contract boundary; edge (WAF/ALB) rate limit throttles abusive volume on the gRPC/ConnectRPC + signup/login surfaces. (Also satisfies the auth+data production-floor abuse-surface item.)
4. `e2e-acceptance` — walks the §8 success-metric journey end-to-end (S1 signup/describe -> S2 tenant boot+publish within latency envelope -> S3 add customer -> S4 create+download+send invoice -> S5 schedule job+advance status -> S6 reminder reaches customer), asserting each ordered step.

## Decision needed
For each: author the formula, OR narrow the plan's guarantee so the concern is honestly `excluded`/covered by an existing feature. Either way is a design call.

## Provenance
Found by smbuild plan-creation co-evo dry-run /decompose (Phase 3.5 missing-formula). Escalate-class.
notes: DECIDED (human, 2026-05-29, interactive /build-next): AUTHOR ALL 4 enforcement formulas — concern-enforcement-abuse-surface, e2e-acceptance, concern-enforcement-data-lifecycle, concern-enforcement-perf. Posture for abuse-surface from noh decision (a). Each modeled on otel-bootstrap-rust shape (Cargo/middleware steps + real integration test, T-14 no-mocks). Wire each into DEFAULT_STACK formula-selection table; verify decompose Phase 3.5 floor->formula resolution picks them up.
close_reason: DECIDED (author-all-4, human 2026-05-29) + implemented (commit a85815d): authored concern-enforcement-abuse-surface, concern-enforcement-data-lifecycle, concern-enforcement-perf, e2e-acceptance. Linked into ~/.beads/formulas/; validated via bd mol pour --dry-run (all pour clean: 5/4/5/4 issues). /decompose Phase 3.5 resolves each by purpose (dynamic bd formula list match — no decompose.js change needed); realizations documented in DEFAULT_STACK §Floor enforcement formulas. Gate PASSED.

title: /decompose does not bless the Jankurai baseline (agent/baselines/main.repo-score.json) -> /build-batch hard-fails GATE_REQUIRE_BASELINE
description: smbuild build-now 2026-05-30: 3 beads that reached the gate hard-failed 'no blessed Jankurai baseline'. post-build-gate.sh L364 + build-next SKILL.md L191 both state an app repo's baseline must be blessed by /decompose before the loop runs; its absence is a /decompose bug. decompose.js creates no baseline. Workaround applied: manually blessed smbuild's baseline via 'jankurai audit . --json agent/baselines/main.repo-score.json' (score 45) + committed (smbuild cfb950f). FIX: /decompose (or a documented post-decompose kickoff) should run the whole-repo jankurai audit and commit agent/baselines/main.repo-score.json as part of producing a BLESSED app, so /build-batch's GATE_REQUIRE_BASELINE=1 is satisfiable. Acceptance: after /decompose on a fresh app, agent/baselines/main.repo-score.json exists + is committed.
notes: ESCALATE (design conflict with igu.2; premise partly incorrect — needs human call): VERIFIED decompose.js Phase 8 (L1058-1110) DOES create+commit agent/baselines/main.repo-score.json, but ONLY on blessed && !dryRun && phase==1 (igu.2): it runs 'jankurai audit . --json agent/baselines/main.repo-score.json', validates the score, fixes .gitignore, and commits it ('chore: accept initial jankurai baseline (blessed at decompose)'). So 'decompose creates no baseline' is false for the BLESSED path — l4z's AC is already met there. The smbuild failure was a NEEDS-FIX decompose (kwk/7ep missing enforcement formulas): igu.2 DELIBERATELY blesses no baseline on NEEDS-FIX (a never-blessed floor on a non-passing plan was the lbq.14 trap; see memory jankurai-witness-floor-trap + igu2-baseline-restamp-design). Building a NEEDS-FIX decompose via build-now is the actual anomaly. HUMAN CALL (l4z's literal 'always create baseline' would REGRESS igu.2): (a) keep igu.2, make build-batch's GATE_REQUIRE_BASELINE failure ACTIONABLE — fail fast in Phase 0 preflight with 'no blessed baseline: decompose blesses only on BLESSED; if overriding a NEEDS-FIX, manually bless via jankurai audit . --json agent/baselines/main.repo-score.json && commit' (the workaround smbuild used, cfb950f); or (b) intentionally bless on a NEEDS-FIX build-now override too (regresses igu.2 — needs explicit sign-off). Not self-implemented: conflicts with a deliberate prior decision.

title: [from-app:smbuild] DAG lacks skeleton-first deps: compiled-surface feature beads not blocked-by app-skeleton foundation -> ratchet rejects every first-Rust-surface feature
description:
OBSERVED (smbuild build-batch drain wf_6e6980d3, BUILD-NOW): wave-planner picked leaf feature smbuild-mol-14bu (postmark HTTP client) to build first. Worker implemented it fully + green (cargo test PASS). Post-merge Jankurai ratchet BLOCKED the merge: introducing the FIRST compiled Rust surface into the greenfield worktree (base cfb950f = zero source tree) activates dormant repo-wide ops/CI caps (no-security-lane, generated-contracts-drift, no-secret-scanning, no-jankurai-CI-lane) -> score 45->48 -> regression block. Worker's own diagnosis: these caps belong to the app-skeleton epic (smbuild-mol-o3h children: grh repo-structure / 0x4 Cargo workspace root / amt agent-metadata+baseline / jmh rustfmt+clippy+deny / 1kn CI). ROOT CAUSE: /decompose did NOT add a dependency from compiled-surface feature beads to the app-skeleton foundation beads, AND build-batch's wave-planner does not topologically prioritize foundation beads. So features sort into ○ autonomous-build-5fb ● P1 [bug] build-batch.js: durationMs ReferenceError in final return (regression of 4ez)
○ autonomous-build-jw9 ● P2 [epic] Triage drain (2026-05-29)
○ autonomous-build-mvh ● P1 [epic] workflows migration: skills to dynamic workflows
○ autonomous-build-mvh.1 ● P2 [epic] /decompose dynamic workflow (subsumes compose+quality-pass+split)
○ autonomous-build-mvh.2 ● P2 [epic] /build-batch dynamic workflow (convert from skill)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Ready: 5 issues with no active blockers

Status: ○ open  ◐ in_progress  ● blocked  ✓ closed  ❄ deferred alongside/ahead of the skeleton (all P1), build-batch builds a feature first, ratchet correctly rejects it -> 0 merges possible until the skeleton lands + baseline re-stamped. ESCALATE-CLASS design decision (do NOT guess): fix is one of (a) /decompose adds skeleton->feature deps; (b) build-batch wave-planner prioritizes the app-skeleton epic's children first; (c) operator manually builds+merges the skeleton beads, re-stamps baseline, then re-dispatches features. Affects EVERY greenfield app, not just smbuild. DEFERRED per BUILD-NOW runbook (capture now, fix is the post-drain feedback loop).
notes: ESCALATE-CLASS (per bead; DEFERRED — post-drain feedback loop; no AC). Skeleton-first ordering gap, affects EVERY greenfield app: /decompose adds no dep from compiled-surface feature beads to the app-skeleton foundation beads, AND build-batch's wave-planner doesn't topologically prioritize foundation beads. So a leaf feature (e.g. smbuild's postmark HTTP client) builds FIRST; introducing the first compiled Rust surface into a zero-source tree activates dormant repo-wide ops/CI caps (no-security-lane, generated-contracts-drift, no-secret-scanning, no-jankurai-CI-lane), score 45->48, post-merge ratchet regression-blocks the merge -> 0 merges until the skeleton lands + baseline re-stamps. Verified gap still present (no skeleton prioritization in build-batch.js / no skeleton deps in decompose.js). HUMAN DESIGN CALL (one of): (a) /decompose wires app-skeleton-epic -> feature deps; (b) build-batch wave-planner builds the app-skeleton epic's children first; (c) operator builds+merges skeleton beads, re-stamps baseline, then re-dispatches features.
close_reason: stale/subsumed: skeleton-ordering fix landed via epic onv. onv.1 (vision stamps build-order tier on featureOrder), onv.2+onv.4 (decompose wires epic-ordering/cross-tier edges + pure-JS topology assertion gate), onv.3 (rework-to-bar loop) all closed. decompose.js now derives FEATURE_TIERS (foundational=app-skeleton,otel) and wires cross-tier deps so compiled-surface feature beads are blocked-by the app-skeleton foundation — directly addressing l6z root cause. No AC (triage capture); design decision resolved (approach: decompose deps + vision tier order, not a build-batch wave-planner change). Remaining verification tracked by onv.5 (re-vision+re-decompose smbuild).

title: Improvements from pipeline-intent critique (2026-05-28)

Children grouped A-E + themes.

title: install: beads-builder agent dispatched but never installed

title: gate: treats 'no tests' as a pass
description: post-build-gate.ps1:43,56 guard on $scripts.test / Get-Command pytest; absence is indistinguishable from green - a mechanical escape hatch under retry pressure. Fix: require a discovered, non-empty test suite per bead or hard-fail.

Flagship Rust-workspace stack has NO Rust branch so the gate runs essentially nothing at repo root. Fix: coverage floor, run integration/e2e in the gate, add SCA, add a Cargo branch.

title: gate: Jankurai enforcer is advisory by default (ratchet disabled, baseline ships {})
description: post-build-gate.ps1:101-104 - the hard-fail witness ratchet is disabled until a human accepts a baseline that ships as {}. The marquee enterprise enforcer has zero blocking power during exactly the unattended window. Fix: populate a real baseline during decompose and make the ratchet live.

comments.author: Sally
comments.text: SUPERSEDED by the autonomous-build-igu epic (Tier the Jankurai gate). lbq.14 made the gate enforce via 'jankurai witness' exit code against an auto-stamped sub-85 scaffold baseline. Review (2 rounds, docs/JANKURAI_GATING_PROPOSAL.md) proved witness BAKES IN the absolute 85 floor — it blocks an identical, zero-delta tree — so the next real app build would deadlock on its first commits (ready>0, no human-clearable cause). The fix (igu.1) replaces witness-exit-code enforcement with a regression-only ratchet parsed from the audit receipt's decision.ratchet (block on score_delta<-2 / new_hard_findings / new_caps, never the floor); igu.2 moves baseline acceptance onto the BLESSED gate + adds the high-water re-stamp; igu.3 wires GATE_REQUIRE_BASELINE=1 in app mode. title: decompose: no vision<->plan coverage check (BLESSED only verifies plan<->DAG)
description: Phase-6 adversarial cross-check verifies plan<->DAG only (decompose.js:574-602); nothing re-reads vision.md. A must-have dropped during /vision distillation is invisible: a 12-must-have vision can build as a faithful 9-feature app with a green report. title: plan-lock: NFRs evaporate to prose tenets (no schema field, never gated)

'Data stays in my region' becomes a sentence, never a bead with an AC and a test, never scored, never gated. Fix: model NFRs as first-class plan-lock entries that pour enforcement beads.

title: decompose: section-8 success metric is never an executable test
description: The user's one definition of done has no schema field and no bead; the cross-feature journey is never verified as a path. Fix: pour an e2e acceptance bead from the vision success metric.

title: build-batch: failed beads send no notification (only blocked does)
description: build-batch.js:393-398 - failed beads (the most unexpected outcome) produce only orphaned worktrees + a log nobody reads; blocked notifies. The severe case is the silent one. Fix: notify on failed too.

title: escalation: gate-fails-twice -> permanent block is a thin retry budget
description: ESCALATION_RULES.md:15 - one retry biases surviving output toward easy beads, strands hard/load-bearing ones. Fix: reconsider retry budget / escalation policy for load-bearing beads.

title: orchestrator: no end-to-end driver for vision->decompose->build-batch->retro
description: Nothing sequences the four stages; /loop only iterates /build-next within the build stage (README.md:20, ARCHITECTURE.md:43). Human is the orchestrator at every seam except build->retro. Fix: top-level build-app driver that takes a filled vision.md and sequences all four stages with checkpoints it can clear autonomously.

title: build-batch: merge conflicts permanently block with no rebase/retry
description: build-batch.js:414-421 - shared config/barrel/lockfiles accumulate over the run, so conflict-blocks rise exactly when the human is furthest away. Fix: auto-rebase + re-run gate before blocking on merge conflict.

title: retro: spec exists but no retro.js/skill; build-next auto-invokes nothing
description: /retro has a spec but no retro.js or skill, yet build-next auto-invokes it - the terminal stage resolves to nothing. Blast radius is the self-improvement loop. Fix: author retro.js per retro.spec.md.

title: docs: drift - build-next cites removed /compose, build-batch.js refs undistributed spec
description: build-next/SKILL.md still cites the removed /compose stage; build-batch.js agent prompts still reference build-batch.spec.md (the bug already fixed in decompose.js). Fix: scrub stale references.

title: vision: front-load auth/secrets/migration decisions so touches-auth beads clear unattended
description: ESCALATION_RULES.md:9-12 hard-stops on auth/secrets/migration; auth-email-password.formula labels all 6 steps touches-auth; build-batch.spec.md:213-214 blocks them unconditionally. A login-bearing app blocks mid-run and pages an absent human. Fix: capture auth-model + secrets choices at human-present /vision time into plan.lock so labeled beads clear unattended.

title: build-batch: no auto-resume after a block (loop exits, only human restart)
description: /escalate sends PushNotification and the loop exits; only restart is a human typing /loop /build-next (escalate/SKILL.md:38,52, ESCALATION_RULES.md:40-41). After drain-to-blocked nothing runs for the rest of the 2 days. Fix: polling driver that re-checks bd ready on interval and resumes when unblocked (or budget/timeout fires).

title: build-batch: --budget parsed but never enforced
description: Flag appears only at build-batch.js:28,34, never read in the 519-line wave loop. The one financial safety stop for an unattended run does not exist. Fix: wire cumulative-cost accounting into the dispatch loop per build-batch.spec.md:425.

title: build-batch: hung worker stalls the whole batch forever (no per-worker timeout)
description: build-batch.js:362 await parallel(buildTasks) has no timeout; spec's 30-min stageTimeout/TaskStop (build-batch.spec.md:411-415) never implemented. A wedged worker never returns so parallel() never resolves: silent infinite stall, no notification. Fix: wrap each worker thunk in the spec'd per-worker timeout.

title: build-batch: crash mid-batch is unrecoverable (all state in-memory)
description: Declared a non-goal (build-batch.spec.md:101,440); over a 2-day window an OOM/restart is likely and strands in_progress beads invisible to bd ready. Fix: checkpoint merged/blocked/failed sets to disk + stale-claim reaper on re-run.

title: decompose: inject a mandatory production floor (not opt-in per-feature)
description: Only app-skeleton is always poured. Auth/authz(openfga)/otel/audit/tenancy/IaC enter only if a vision must-have happens to map to them, and templates/vision.md asks for product features not infra. Example vision yields skeleton+crud+maybe-auth. Fix: mandatory production-floor formula set for any app declaring auth/data, with decompose fidelity check aware of it.

title: deploy: nothing is ever deployed (terraform plan-only, all apply steps human-gated)
description: terraform-aws-baseline writes HCL / at best terraform plan; state-bucket bootstrap (line 109), secrets (581), and apply all require a human. No smoke test, health check, SLO, alerting, backup verification. User gets a git repo that passes tests, not a running app. Fix: unattended deploy-to-dev path (CI-issued creds, auto-created state backend) + post-deploy health/smoke bead.

title: PLAN_CONCERNS.md + SKILL.md: drop 'budget' from the secrets/data-lifecycle elevator (oracle says budget alone does not elevate)
description:
vision.js deriveApplicability (autonomous-build-ih5.2) elevates secrets only on authn|external-integrations|privacy, and data-lifecycle only on privacy|PII — NOT on a budget constraint. The eval oracle (tests/vision-eval/fixtures/04-public-unauth-api) establishes that a §6 budget line is a cost ceiling, not a secret-management signal; elevating secrets on budget there would manufacture a false required+excluded contradiction (oracle expectIncomplete=false, secrets=excluded).

But docs/PLAN_CONCERNS.md §'Applicability' (secrets row: 'authn or external-integrations is required, OR §6 has a privacy/budget constraint') and skills/vision/SKILL.md step 8.5/8 ('§6 (a privacy/budget/infra constraint -> secrets + data-lifecycle)') still say 'budget'. The prose disagrees with the oracle + the now-shipped derivation.

FIX: drop 'budget' from the secrets elevator in both docs (a paid-API budget surfaces as external-integrations instead, which already elevates secrets). Confirm 'infra' too. Keep deriveApplicability and the docs in sync (the same const-sync rule). vision.js already implements + comments the oracle-grounded behavior; this is the doc side of the reconciliation.

Found while building ih5.2.

title: Confirm-the-fix: from-app beads re-validate against originating app before close
description: The reverse leg. A from-app:<X> bead carries the originating repro command and goes to needs-app-confirm (not closed) until that step re-runs green against app X. Formalizes what repro-smbuild-decompose-run2.md did by hand as the provable loop-closed moment. Could be a /confirm-upstream <bead> mini-skill or a build-next post-step for from-app beads.
design: Shape decided (owner, 2026-05-29): mini-skill + convention (NOT a build-next post-step — that auto-route is a deferred follow-up). skills/confirm-upstream/SKILL.md is the discrete, human/loop-invokable tool; AGENTS.md documents the needs-app-confirm convention + from-app bead lifecycle. Reverse path resolution (meta->app X) is a sibling-probe validated by .git present AND skills/build-next/SKILL.md absent.
acceptance_criteria: skills/confirm-upstream/SKILL.md exists and: (1) reads a from-app:<X> bead + extracts its repro; (2) resolves app X via sibling-probe (--app-path override) validated against the meta repo; (3) re-runs the repro safely (destructive/slash-command repros -> needs-app-confirm, never the live checkout); (4) closes ONLY on green (Actual==Expected) recording 'confirmed-fixed vs X@sha', else holds at needs-app-confirm. AGENTS.md documents the needs-app-confirm convention + two-stage from-app close. Installers auto-link the new skill dir (skills/*/ glob).

title: workflows migration: skills to dynamic workflows
description: Convert compose/quality-pass/split skills into a single /decompose dynamic workflow, and convert build-batch into a /build-batch dynamic workflow. End state: one umbrella decompose command produces a blessed atomic bead DAG with adversarial fidelity cross-check vs plan.md; one build-batch workflow fans out builders and serializes the merger. Old skills deleted after new workflows ship and docs are updated. Human gate between /decompose and /build-batch preserved.

title: /decompose dynamic workflow (subsumes compose+quality-pass+split)
description: Umbrella workflow that takes plan.md and produces a blessed atomic bead DAG. Phases: parse plan -> fan-out pour beads per feature -> fan-out atomize oversized beads -> fan-out quality-score each epic -> adversarial fidelity cross-check (two agents independently compare DAG vs plan.md, both must agree) -> topological dep audit. Output: decomposeReport.md + BLESSED|NEEDS-FIX verdict. Replaces /compose, /quality-pass, /split.

title: A1: write workflows/decompose.spec.md
description: Author the design doc for the /decompose dynamic workflow. Structure mirrors workflows/retro.spec.md: header, how-this-workflow-runs (hand-authored JS pattern, NOT first-run-generated), inputs (--plan path, --since/--until, --no-file dry-run), phases (parse, pour, atomize, quality, fidelity-cross-check, dep-audit), per-phase agent specs (name, tools, inputs, outputs, failure), run-completion behavior, stopping conditions, do-not list. The spec.md is the contract; A2 implements it. Acceptance: workflows/decompose.spec.md exists; every phase has named agents with declared tools and IO; the fidelity cross-check phase explicitly describes the two-verifier-then-reconcile pattern with disagreement handling.

title: A2: write workflows/decompose.js
description: Hand-author the canonical JS script that implements workflows/decompose.spec.md. The script orchestrates subagents per the dynamic-workflow API (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/workflows). All phases declared in the spec must be present: parse, pour (fan-out per feature), atomize (fan-out per oversized bead, loops until atomic), quality (fan-out per epic), fidelity-cross-check (2 verifiers per concern + reconciler), dep-audit (topo sort + cycle detect). Intermediate state lives in JS variables, not Claude's context. Acceptance: workflows/decompose.js exists; install.ps1 -DryRun shows it would be hardlinked to ~/.claude/workflows/; the script's phase structure matches the spec one-to-one.

title: A3: smoke test /decompose on a real plan.md
description: End-to-end run of /decompose against a real plan.md fixture (pick an existing sibling app with a plan.md, or fabricate a 5-feature plan.md in workflows/test/decompose-fixture.md). Verify: (1) pour phase creates the expected number of root beads; (2) atomize phase splits any bead with >6 ACs; (3) quality phase emits scores and remediations; (4) fidelity cross-check runs both verifiers and reconciles; (5) blessed verdict emitted iff all checks pass AND every plan feature has at least one bead. Capture transcript or /workflows progress-view output. Acceptance: smoke-test results recorded in workflows/test/decompose.smoketest.md OR in the bead notes; the run produced a non-empty decomposeReport.md; no phase errored.
notes: Smoke test requires user-initiated invocation of /decompose against a fresh app repo (decompose.spec.md Phase 1 refuses if the repo has open beads, so it cannot run against autonomous-build itself). I cannot dispatch /decompose from inside /build-next — slash commands are user-driven, and the workflow runtime is not exposed as a tool to subagents. SMBuild (../SMBuild/) is the natural smoke-test target: it has plan.md, plan.lock.json, vision.md, tenets.md, and no .beads/ directory. To complete this bead the human should: (1) cd ../SMBuild; (2) ./install.ps1 in autonomous-build first to hardlink workflows/decompose.js into ~/.claude/workflows/; (3) /decompose --no-file (dry run) to verify all 8 phases complete without mutating bd; (4) inspect decomposeReport.md; (5) if all phases ran and report is non-empty, write findings to autonomous-build/workflows/test/decompose.smoketest.md (transcript or summary of phase outcomes + verdict); (6) close this bead. If /decompose --no-file passes, also run /decompose live to verify the mutation path (Phase 2 bd init + jankurai adopt, Phase 3 pours actually happen).

title: A4: delete skills/compose, skills/quality-pass, skills/split

Acceptance: skills/compose/, skills/quality-pass/, skills/split/ no longer exist in the repo; running install.ps1 reports the stale junctions as cleaned; no remaining references to these skill paths in any repo file (verified by grep).

Verified by git grep: no live source references to these skill paths remain — only the dated retro (retros/retro-autonomous-build-2026-05-28.md, historical git-analysis record, must not be rewritten) and workflows/decompose.spec.md's migration-rationale notes (lines 10/399, which intentionally document the replacement and this deletion). install.ps1 stale-junction cleanup NOT run here: junctions are an NTFS/Windows concept and this session is Linux — defer to the user's next Windows ./install.ps1 run, which will drop the ~/.claude/skills/{compose,quality-pass,split} junctions automatically.

title: /build-batch dynamic workflow (convert from skill)
description: Convert skills/build-batch/SKILL.md from an in-conversation orchestration skill into a dynamic-workflow JS script. Phases: preflight (meta-guard + bd-healthy + main-clean + ready beads exist) -> build-and-merge loop (fan-out builders up to --workers, serialized merger with post-merge gate, filesTouched conflict filter) -> summary + auto-invoke /escalate if blocked. Hand-authored JS in workflows/build-batch.js, design rationale in workflows/build-batch.spec.md.

title: B1: write workflows/build-batch.spec.md
description: Author the design doc for the /build-batch dynamic workflow. Port the existing skills/build-batch/SKILL.md sections into spec.md shape (matching workflows/retro.spec.md structure). Preserve every semantic from the existing skill: meta-mode guard in preflight phase, --workers/--max-merges/--budget flags, filesTouched conflict filter, BUILD_COMPLETE marker file at .bd-build-complete.json, serialized merger with post-merge gate, in-memory pipeline state expressed as script variables, structured BATCH COMPLETE summary, auto-invoke /escalate on blocked, optional /retro on full drain. The spec is the design rationale; B2 implements it. Acceptance: workflows/build-batch.spec.md exists; every section from the old SKILL is represented; meta-mode guard documented as Phase 0; no semantics dropped without an explicit Removed: note.

title: B2: write workflows/build-batch.js
description: Hand-author the canonical JS script that implements workflows/build-batch.spec.md. Dynamic-workflow API per https://code.claude.com/docs/en/workflows. Required behaviors: Phase 0 meta-guard agent refuses to proceed if skills/build-next/SKILL.md exists; Phase 1 preflight (bd healthy, main clean, ready beads); Phase 2 build-and-merge loop fans out builders up to --workers concurrent, polls .bd-build-complete.json markers, runs ONE merger agent at a time against main with post-merge gate, applies filesTouched conflict filter when selecting new candidates; Phase 3 summary + auto-invoke /escalate if blockedSet non-empty. Acceptance: workflows/build-batch.js exists; install.ps1 -DryRun shows hardlink target; script never spawns 2+ concurrent mergers; every bead state transition (claim/build/merge/close OR block OR fail) is reachable.

title: B3: smoke test /build-batch in an app repo
description: End-to-end run of /build-batch in a sibling app repo (NOT autonomous-build itself -- meta-mode guard refuses). Pick an app with at least 3 ready non-epic beads. Run /build-batch --workers 2 --max-merges 2 and verify: (1) preflight passes; (2) two builder agents dispatch concurrently in worktrees; (3) merger agent runs after each builder completion, never concurrently with another merger; (4) post-merge gate fires on main; (5) batch terminates cleanly with BATCH COMPLETE summary. Acceptance: smoke-test results in workflows/test/build-batch.smoketest.md OR in the bead notes; transcript shows >=1 successful builder->merger->close cycle; meta-mode guard verified separately by invoking in autonomous-build and confirming refusal.
notes: Smoke test requires user-initiated invocation of /build-batch in a sibling app repo with >=3 ready non-epic beads. I cannot dispatch /build-batch from inside /build-next — slash commands are user-driven, and Phase 0 of /build-batch correctly refuses to run in autonomous-build (skills/build-next/SKILL.md is the meta-mode marker). To complete this bead the human should: (1) ./install.ps1 in autonomous-build to hardlink workflows/build-batch.js into ~/.claude/workflows/; (2) cd to an app repo with >=3 ready non-epic beads (e.g. ../SMBuild after running /decompose to populate beads, or any app currently mid-build); (3) verify the meta-mode guard separately by first running /build-batch in autonomous-build itself and confirming it refuses with 'MODE: meta'; (4) in the app repo, run /build-batch --workers 2 --max-merges 2; (5) confirm: (a) Phase 1 pre-flight passes; (b) two beads-builder agents dispatch concurrently in worktrees; (c) merger runs sequentially (never two concurrent); (d) post-merge gate fires on main between merges; (e) batch terminates with BATCH COMPLETE summary listing merged/blocked/failed sets; (6) write findings to workflows/test/build-batch.smoketest.md (transcript summary + verdict); (7) close this bead. Note: v1 build-batch.js implements wave-dispatch rather than continuous polling — the smoke test validates this is acceptable for v1 or surfaces the need for true poll-loop semantics in a follow-up bead.

title: abuse-surface is a mandatory auth production-floor item but DEFAULT_STACK pins no default rate-limit posture
description: Upstream capture from smbuild (coevo plan-loop tick 10, 2026-05-29). Category: default-stack/concern. Observation: decompose.spec.md L120 makes abuse-surface a MANDATORY production-floor item whenever declaresAuth; the vision abuse-surface concern bar requires an anchored input-validation AND rate-limit stance per public surface. But docs/DEFAULT_STACK.md L52 only NAMES abuse-surface as a floor concern ('input-validation / rate-limit on exposed surfaces') and pins NO concrete default rate-limit/throttle/WAF posture (verified by grep — none). Result: every auth'd app with a public surface blocks at the vision concern-decidedness gate on a rate-limit decision the human must hand-anchor, even though the floor declares the concern mandatory. PROPOSED: pin a sensible default abuse/rate-limit posture in DEFAULT_STACK (e.g. edge ALB/WAF rate-based rules + per-tenant in-app token-bucket via tower-governor) that vision can anchor to, overridable per-app — same pattern as the observability/audit/iac floor defaults. DISCUSSABLE (asking the human may be correct for threat-model reasons) — vet before promoting. Evidence: decompose.spec.md L120; DEFAULT_STACK.md L52; smbuild plan.lock openQuestion; coevo journal tick 10.
notes: REOPENED: the DEFAULT_STACK.md pin (commit 305a130) is necessary but NOT sufficient. vision.js inlines DEFAULT_STACK (line 105) as 'The stack + Stack-native formulas' ONLY — the production-floor/abuse-surface posture is not inlined, and the Phase-3 concern agent (concernPrompt) receives its concern bar + EVIDENCE_BAR but NOT the pinned stack, so it still cannot cite the posture and still blocks at the decidedness gate (the exact symptom this bead names). Remaining work: propagate the pin into vision's decidedness path (inline the floor posture into vision.js DEFAULT_STACK const + feed the pinned stack to the concern agent), in lockstep with vision.spec.md (T3) + check the vision-eval corpus.
close_reason: Completed (commits 305a130 + this): (1) pinned the abuse-surface posture in DEFAULT_STACK; (2) propagated it into vision's decidedness path — enriched the abuse-surface concern bar to name the posture + concern-enforcement-abuse-surface formula (lockstep PLAN_CONCERNS.md + vision.js CONCERN_BARS), so an auth'd/public app cites the DEFAULT_STACK pin as evidence and resolves abuse-surface 'addressed' instead of blocking. Matches the existing observability/authn bar pattern. vision selftest 82/82; gate PASSED. Fixes fixtures 01/05/06 (abuse-surface required + expectIncomplete:false + blocking:[]).

Stale 'gastownhall/beads' references in docs/INSTALL.md and README.md corrected in same change.

title: Reliability hardening from Gas City borrow analysis (2026-05-30)
description: Borrow runtime-survival mechanisms surfaced by the grounded gascity comparison (docs/gascity-borrow-analysis-2026-05-30.md). Six source-grounded, daemon-free fixes to build-batch.js / decompose.js (+ specs) that close real long-run failure modes: lost finished work, poison-bead loops, transient-blip aborts, silent partial success, false drains, late cycle detection.

title: build-batch: salvage already-merged orphans on reap (close, don't re-dispatch)
description: execute-2 (highest leverage). The crash-recovery reaper (build-batch.js ~L195-200) git branch -D's every orphan and the merge agent bd close's LAST (L592), so a crash between merge (L578) and close strands an already-merged bead: reaper re-opens it and a worker wastefully rebuilds it (or permanently strands it on repeat). Fix: before reopen, if branch bead/<id> is an ancestor of main (git merge-base --is-ancestor), bd close it and skip re-dispatch. Local refs only. Defer the keep-branch-and-re-gate salvage. See gascity examples/.../mol-witness-patrol.toml. Touches: workflows/build-batch.js + build-batch.spec.md.

title: build-batch: durable reap-count crash-loop quarantine for poison beads
description: execute-1. A bead that hard-crashes the runtime mid-worker (OOM/restart) is reaped to open and re-picked forever, silently burning the window. Fix: reaper reads+increments a durable metadata.reaped counter (read-merge-write the full metadata object to preserve filesTouched); at >= K (3) set --status=blocked with a crash-loop note so it routes to /escalate instead of looping. Use durable bd metadata, not an in-memory window. See gascity cmd/gc/crash_tracker.go. Touches: workflows/build-batch.js + spec.

title: build-batch: store-health probe before treating 0-candidates as drain
description: robustness-5. The 0-candidates path (build-batch.js L378-383) just breaks, so a mid-run jsonl lock / --db-at-dir blindness false-drains into 'done' (and /retro) when the store merely went blind. Fix: at the 0-candidates juncture, probe store health (bd ready --json; bd doctor --fix once on anomaly); if still unhealthy -> [BATCH ABORT] with a distinct reason (not retro-suggested). Drop gascity's Dolt MB/row metric (jsonl). See gascity internal/storehealth + internal/doctor. Touches: workflows/build-batch.js + spec.

title: decompose: pre-pour cycle check over crossFeatureDependencies
description: decompose-3. A cyclic crossFeatureDependencies set survives to Phase 7 assertTopology, failing NEEDS-FIX only AFTER a full pour+wiring pass mutated bd. Fix: a ~18-line pure-JS colour-DFS cycle detector (mirroring assertTopology decompose.js:1180-1195) over ParsedPlan.crossDeps, run after Parse (L210) before Pour; on a cycle return the existing {verdict:'NEEDS-FIX', phase:'pre-pour-cycle-check', ...} early-return shape before any bd mutation. Scope to cross-feature edges. See gascity internal/formula/compile.go orderGraphRecipeSteps. Touches: workflows/decompose.js + spec.

title: decompose: transient-vs-permanent retry on the pour path
description: decompose-4. A single transient jsonl/db lock error on bd mol pour (the concurrent-checkout contention) drops the whole run to NEEDS-FIX with no retry; the pour path names only permanent causes (required-var/off-enum/formula-not-found). Fix: classify transient bd error text (database is locked / lock / i/o timeout / resource busy) and sleep-and-retry once (mirroring build-batch.js:103), keeping permanent causes as immediate fails. See gascity internal/molecule isTransientGraphApplyError. Touches: workflows/decompose.js + spec.

title: decompose: post-pour step-completeness assertion
description: decompose-2. bd mol pour can exit 0 but spawn a subset of declared steps; pourFailed===0 passes and the deficit only maybe surfaces in the probabilistic Phase 6 verifiers. Fix: pour agent returns its formula's substituted, condition-filtered expected step-title set; a pure-JS expected-but-absent assertion (normalized compare) forces NEEDS-FIX on any deficit. Honor condition/needs filtering (avoid false positives); skip dry-run. Threads a new failure signal into the Phase 8 verdict. See gascity internal/beads/graph_apply.go ValidateGraphApplyResult. Touches: workflows/decompose.js + spec.

title: decompose returns reportPath null even though decomposeReport.md was written
description: Source: smbuild /decompose run 2026-05-28. Evidence: the workflow final result reported reportPath=null and failedPhases included nothing about the report, yet decomposeReport.md was actually written to the app cwd (10677 bytes). The write-report agent path was not propagated into finalResult. Verification: final task result JSON (reportPath null) versus the existing decomposeReport.md on disk.
acceptance_criteria: workflows/decompose.js Phase 8 sets finalResult.reportPath to the path returned by the write-report agent whenever the write succeeds.
close_reason: write-report agent now declares a return schema {status,reportPath,verdict,reportMarkdown?}, so reportResult is structured and finalResult.reportPath reflects the written path instead of always being null. Added a loud-failure log that surfaces the inlined markdown if the write fails. Spec Phase 8 updated.

title: Validate converted formulas end-to-end with bd cook + bd mol pour
description: After formulas are converted to bd's TOML schema (sibling bead), prove they actually work end-to-end. Today the /compose skill assumes a working bd cook + bd mol pour pipeline but it has never been tested against the current bd build. WHAT TO DO: (1) in a scratch project (e.g. /tmp/bd-pour-test or a temp dir), bd init + copy the converted formulas in. (2) run bd cook app-skeleton --mode=runtime --var app_name=demo --var language=typescript --var framework=nextjs --var test_framework=vitest --var lint_tool=biome — verify the proto output is well-formed. (3) bd mol pour <proto-id> --parent <epic-id> — verify the spawned issues have correct titles, types, descriptions, and dependency edges (needs:). (4) repeat for crud-feature with a realistic entity. (5) bd ready should return the leaf tasks. (6) if anything fails or behaves unexpectedly, document the deviation in formulas/README.md and either fix the formula or escalate. Depends on the formula-conversion bead.

title: /compose: bd mol pour has no --parent flag; reparent via bd dep add after pour
description: /compose documents 'bd mol pour <proto-id> --parent <epic-id>' but bd 0.55.3's mol pour command has no --parent flag (verified during autonomous-build-s9f validation). Pouring spawns its own root epic; to put it under the app-level epic, reparent after pouring. WHAT TO DO: in skills/compose/SKILL.md Step 4, replace the two-step 'bd cook --persist + bd mol pour --parent' pattern with a one-step 'bd mol pour <formula-name> --var ...' followed by 'bd dep add <pour-root-id> <app-epic-id> --type parent-child'. Capture the pour-root-id from pour's output. Also: drop the unnecessary cook --persist intermediate — pour accepts formula names directly.

title: Add installer script that junctions autonomous-build/skills/* into ~/.claude/skills/
description: Currently every new skill in autonomous-build/skills/ requires a manual 'mklink /J ~/.claude/skills/<name> ...' step before Claude Code can invoke it as a slash command. Easy to forget; caught when /quality-pass (autonomous-build-74i) wasn't immediately invocable. WHAT TO DO: add a small installer (PowerShell .ps1 or .cmd, since junctions are Windows-only and the workflow targets Windows per the shell config) that walks autonomous-build/skills/*, for each subdir checks whether ~/.claude/skills/<name> exists and points at the right target, and creates the missing junction with 'mklink /J' otherwise. Idempotent — re-running is safe. Document the invocation in README.md (probably 'Setup' section). Worth checking whether the same problem exists for autonomous-build/formulas/ → ~/.beads/formulas/ (it might already be junctioned but a future formula won't auto-link).

title: Upstream capture: /flag --upstream + meta-path resolution + bd-create label fix

(2) docs/META_PATH_RESOLUTION.md: env -> installed-skill-link trace -> candidate probe (validates .beads/ + skills/build-next/SKILL.md). (3) /retro --meta-path default was a nonexistent hardcoded path (silent file-only/zero-bead runs) -> now resolved via the same rule, loud on unresolved. (4) bd create takes --labels not --add-label; fixed in retro.js + retro.spec.md + AGENTS.md (auto-filer would have dumped every bead to FAILED-TO-FILE). Files: skills/flag/SKILL.md, workflows/retro.js, workflows/retro.spec.md, docs/META_PATH_RESOLUTION.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md.

title: Convert formulas to bd canonical TOML schema
description: The autonomous-build formulas use a YAML schema that bd 0.55.3 does not recognize. bd cook expects .formula.json or .formula.toml. Canonical schema differs from current YAML in several ways: top-level field is 'formula' not 'name'; variables go in [vars.<name>] tables; step deps use 'needs:' not 'depends_on:'; bd has no separate 'acceptance:' field (must be embedded in description); top-level 'type = workflow' and 'version = 1' are required. WHAT TO DO: (1) rewrite formulas/crud-feature.formula.yaml and formulas/app-skeleton.formula.yaml as .formula.toml files matching the canonical bd schema (use ~/Documents/Github/beads/.beads/formulas/beads-release.formula.toml as reference). (2) decide whether to embed the acceptance criteria inside each step's description as a clear 'Acceptance:' block, or to extend bd. (3) delete the .yaml files after conversion. (4) update formulas/README.md to describe the actual TOML schema, removing the speculative YAML schema sketch. Discovered 2026-05-28 while trying to add testPlan fields per autonomous-build-a2m.

title: vision: stop asking human tech questions; default to Jankurai stack; agent-consult off-stack
description:
During /vision plan phase, do NOT ask the user any technical/stack questions. Always default to the canonical Jankurai stack:

- Rust core
- TypeScript + React + Vite product surface
- PostgreSQL as truth
- Generated contracts (TS<->Rust types)
- Exception-only Python for AI/data service (never for repo tooling, product truth, or general backend glue)

For technical decisions outside this stack (e.g. a formula gap, a novel integration choice), spawn a parallel team of 3 agents (architect / reviewer / counter-arguer), synthesize their consensus, and record the decision in plan.md as 'Decided by agent consult'. Never surface tech as an open question for the human.

The 'Open questions for human' section in plan.md is reserved for *product/scope* ambiguities only (contradictory must-haves, missing success metric, undefined non-goals).

Acceptance:
- skills/vision/SKILL.md updated: process step 'Resolve every no-preference with a recommendation' replaced with 'Default to Jankurai stack always; never ask tech questions; agent-consult for off-stack decisions'
- A docs/DEFAULT_STACK.md exists with the canonical stack written out and referenced from the vision skill
- templates/vision.md §7 (Tech preferences) removed or replaced with a note that tech is auto-resolved
- Stopping conditions in vision skill no longer block on tech ambiguity; only product/scope
close_reason: Implemented: vision skill no longer asks tech questions. Docs/DEFAULT_STACK.md pins the Jankurai stack (Rust core + TS/React/Vite + Postgres + generated contracts + exception-only Python). Vision skill step 7 routes off-stack tech decisions to a 3-agent consult (architect/reviewer/counter-arguer), records in plan.md under 'Decided by agent consult'. plan.lock schema gains agentConsults[] field; PLAN_LOCK.md docs the field. templates/vision.md §7 neutered to point at DEFAULT_STACK.md.

title: composer-grammar-version.formula.toml: [vars.version] has both required=true and default -> bd 0.55.4 rejects, pour fails
description:
## Summary
`formulas/composer-grammar-version.formula.toml` (lines ~49-52) declares `[vars.version]` with BOTH `required = true` AND `default = "1"`. bd 0.55.4 disallows this ("cannot have both required:true and default"), so the pour of the "Closed-grammar composer schema v1" feature fails during /decompose. Var-binding validation itself passed; the defect is in the formula TOML.

## Fix
Drop `required = true` from `[vars.version]` (keep the default) OR drop the default (keep required). Re-pour to confirm.

## Provenance
Found by smbuild plan-creation co-evo dry-run /decompose (pour 4 failed). Mechanical formula-authoring defect.
notes: FIX APPLIED (working tree, uncommitted): removed required=true from [vars.version] in formulas/composer-grammar-version.formula.toml, keeping default='1' (defaulted var is satisfiable; matches 'Phase 1 pours version=1'). Verified: tomllib parse OK; bd cook composer-grammar-version --mode=compile exit=0.

title: vision intake: consider surfacing prose-derived candidate must-haves before hard NEEDS-INPUT
description: Upstream capture from smbuild (coevo plan-loop, 2026-05-29). Category: vision-intake. Observation: smbuild/vision.md named its must-have blocks (CRM/Invoicing/Scheduling/Tasks/Comms) and metric (p50<=15min/p95<=30min) in SS1/SS2 PROSE but had no structured SS3/SS8; intake (LOAD_BEARING=problem,mustHaves,successMetric; 'maps by meaning not heading') returned hard NEEDS-INPUT. Arguably correct (template wants discrete SS3 must-haves w/ M-IDs), but consider extracting prose-named candidate capabilities for human confirmation to lower intake friction. LOW CONFIDENCE / debatable — vet before promoting. Evidence: smbuild/vision.md SS1 thesis + SS2 promises; coevo tick logs 165547Z/170200Z.
acceptance_criteria: In workflows/vision.js, the intake stage surfaces prose-derived candidate must-haves before hard NEEDS-INPUT, provable by file inspection: (1) the `intakePrompt` agent prompt (currently ~lines 920-933) instructs the intake agent that when load-bearing prose sections (§1 problem / §2 users) name candidate capabilities but the structured §3 must-haves is empty/placeholder, it must extract those named capabilities and return them under a `candidateMustHaves` array (and the intake output schema, ~lines 1046-1063, declares that key); and (2) the headless needs-input path (`buildNeedsInput`, ~lines 189-202, and/or `validateIntake` ~lines 207-229) includes those extracted candidates in the `missing-product-sections` openQuestion text so the human/shell can confirm them rather than re-typing. Verify: `grep -i "candidateMustHave" workflows/vision.js` returns matches in both the intake prompt and the needs-input/openQuestion construction, and the same candidate-surfacing behavior is documented in workflows/vision.spec.md Phase 1 (~lines 81-82). The repo's existing gate (`node workflows/vision.js --selftest`) still passes. Today none of these strings exist (`grep -ri "candidateMustHave" workflows/` is empty), so the AC reduces to: those instruction/field/openQuestion additions are present in the two synced files and the selftest is green.
close_reason: Closed per human triage: low-confidence/debatable vision-intake enhancement (surface prose-derived candidate must-haves), in the same vision.js intake surface that epic autonomous-build-0ms (0ms.2: '/vision proposes phase split + retains deferred must-haves') will rework. Folding into the 0ms vision rewrite rather than fixing the pre-rewrite code. NOT a 0ms content-duplicate; re-file against the new intake code if the friction persists.

title: /build-next: skip epics in bd ready output
description: bd ready returns epics (which are containers, not work). /build-next would claim an epic, fail to implement it, and waste a tick. bd ready has no --type filter, so the skill has to filter client-side. WHAT TO DO: in skills/build-next/SKILL.md Step 1, change the bd ready call to fetch the full ready list and pick the first non-epic. PowerShell sketch: bd ready --json | ConvertFrom-Json | Where-Object { $_.issue_type -ne 'epic' } | Select-Object -First 1. Empty-result handling stays the same. Caught during the first attempt to run /build-next on autonomous-build's own meta-work, 2026-05-28.

title: Add tenets system: docs/TENETS.md + templates/tenets.md + /vision integration
description: Introduce a workflow-level tenets document (autonomous-build/docs/TENETS.md) that codifies the principles the loop falls back on for build-time judgment calls (escalate over guess, gate is the contract, atomic bead, scope discipline, reversibility bias, formula precedence, failure visibility, idempotency, meta-vs-app, lock-vs-narrative). Make /vision produce a per-app tenets.md alongside plan.md + plan.lock.json that inherits the workflow tenets and adds app-specific tenets derived from vision.md non-goals/constraints + plan.lock agentConsults + deferred openQuestions. Goal: when an agent hits a judgment call during build-next, it has a concrete document to consult.
close_reason: Workflow-level docs/TENETS.md created (10 tenets + source-of-truth ordering + hard-conflict resolutions + non-tenets list). Per-app templates/tenets.md created that inherits T1-T10 and derives A-tenets from vision.md non-goals/constraints + plan.lock agentConsults + deferred openQuestions. skills/vision/SKILL.md updated: new step 9 derives tenets, Outputs section now lists tenets.md alongside plan.md + plan.lock.json, Do-not section forbids asking the user about tenets and forbids paraphrasing T1-T10. README.md pipeline diagram + repo layout updated; CLAUDE.md Orientation updated. Follow-up filed as autonomous-build-2g0 for /build-next consumption.

title: decompose cross-dep wiring reports success but under-applies edges
description: Source: smbuild /decompose run 2026-05-28. Evidence: the Phase 3 cross-dep wiring agent reported applied=24, but the Phase 6 fidelity verifier found only 13 cross-feature edges present, with 9 missing between successfully-poured molecule epics (e.g. grammar to tenant-boot, contracts to customer, customer to property/job). The agent claimed success without confirming edges landed; a likely cause is wiring against pourRoot IDs versus the molecule-epic IDs the verifier checks. Verification: compared the wiring agent log (applied 24) against decomposeReport.md coverage section (13 applied, 15 missing).
acceptance_criteria: workflows/decompose.js Phase 3 cross-dep wiring re-queries each edge via bd dep or bd show after bd dep add and reports the verified-present count, not the attempted count; the dep audit reconciles declared vs present edges between molecule epics.
close_reason: Phase 3 cross-dep wiring agent now re-queries each edge after bd dep add (bd show --json / bd dep show), retries once against visible molecule-epic IDs on a pourRoot-vs-epic mismatch, and reports verifiedPresent (not attempted) plus a missing[] list via a new schema. Spec Phase 3 + Phase 7 updated.

title: tenant-boot-rust.formula.toml: [vars.grammar_version] has both required=true and default="1" -> bd 0.55.4 rejects, tenant-boot pour fails
description:
## Summary
First REAL (non-dry-run) /decompose of smbuild poured 22/23 features; the 'Tenant-boot chokepoint (TenantCtx + boot_tenant + RLS GUC middleware)' feature FAILED to pour. `bd mol pour tenant-boot-rust` fails at formula validation: 'vars.grammar_version: cannot have both required:true and default'. The formula (formulas/tenant-boot-rust.formula.toml, also ~/.beads/formulas/) declares [vars.grammar_version] with BOTH required = true (line 43) AND default = "1" (line 44); bd 0.55.4 rejects the combo, so the formula won't seed as a proto and the pour reports 'tenant-boot-rust not found as formula or proto ID'.

## Impact (forces decompose NEEDS-FIX, two ways)
1. Pour failure: the tenant-boot feature has zero beads.
2. Cascade coverage-gap: concerns 'authz' and 'perf-envelope' cite the tenant-boot feature as their addressed-by; with no implementing bead, concernGap=true -> NEEDS-FIX even though plan<->DAG coverage is otherwise clean.

## Fix
Drop EITHER required = true OR default = "1" on [vars.grammar_version] (keep default to preserve the v1 ships default), then re-pour. Identical bug-class to autonomous-build-xov (composer-grammar-version.formula.toml [vars.version]) — that fix only touched composer-grammar-version; tenant-boot-rust has the same latent defect and was never patched. Worth a grep across all formulas/*.toml for any other [vars.*] with both required=true and default. No beads mutated.

## Provenance
smbuild plan-creation co-evo, first real /decompose (verdict NEEDS-FIX, beadCount 160, appEpic smbuild-14y). Mechanical/meta. Route fix via /build-next gate (do NOT blind-edit; parallel session active on the shared checkout).
